---
title: "Chapter 1: GaN Transistor Fundamentals for RF Power Amplifiers"
subtitle: "Comprehensive Analysis Based on IFX Tx_Baseline Project"
author: "PA Design Reference Manual"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 4
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
      smooth_scroll: true
    number_sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    highlight: kate
    code_folding: hide
    fig_caption: true
    self_contained: true
    css: custom_mobile_styles.css
bibliography: ../../references.bib
---

<style>
/* ========================================
   COLOR SYSTEM - Accessible & Color-Blind Friendly
   ========================================
   
   Color Blind Considerations:
   - Avoiding red-green combinations (most common: deuteranopia/protanopia)
   - Using blue-orange/yellow contrast
   - High contrast ratios (WCAG AA: 4.5:1 for text)
   - Patterns/styles to differentiate beyond color
   ======================================== */

:root {
  /* === Light Mode Colors (Color-Blind Friendly) === */
  
  /* Primary Colors - Blue/Cyan spectrum (safe for color blindness) */
  --primary-blue: #0077be;
  --primary-blue-dark: #005a8d;
  --primary-blue-light: #4a9fd8;
  --primary-blue-pale: #d6ecf7;
  
  /* Secondary Colors - Orange/Amber spectrum (distinguishable from blue) */
  --secondary-orange: #d97706;
  --secondary-orange-dark: #b45309;
  --secondary-orange-light: #f59e0b;
  --secondary-orange-pale: #fef3c7;
  
  /* Tertiary Colors - Purple (neutral for color blindness) */
  --tertiary-purple: #7c3aed;
  --tertiary-purple-dark: #5b21b6;
  --tertiary-purple-light: #a78bfa;
  
  /* Neutral Colors */
  --text-primary: #1a1a1a;
  --text-secondary: #4a4a4a;
  --text-tertiary: #6b7280;
  --bg-primary: #ffffff;
  --bg-secondary: #f3f4f6;
  --bg-tertiary: #e5e7eb;
  --border-color: #d1d5db;
  --border-color-light: #e5e7eb;
  
  /* Semantic Colors (Color-Blind Safe) */
  --success-color: #0891b2; /* Cyan instead of green */
  --warning-color: #ea580c; /* Orange */
  --error-color: #dc2626; /* Red (used sparingly, with icons) */
  --info-color: #0077be; /* Blue */
  
  /* Graph Colors (Color-Blind Friendly Palette) */
  --graph-color-1: #0077be; /* Blue */
  --graph-color-2: #d97706; /* Orange */
  --graph-color-3: #7c3aed; /* Purple */
  --graph-color-4: #0891b2; /* Cyan */
  --graph-color-5: #ea580c; /* Deep Orange */
  --graph-color-6: #475569; /* Slate Gray */
  
  /* Interactive Elements */
  --link-color: #0077be;
  --link-hover: #005a8d;
  --focus-outline: #4a9fd8;
  
  /* Shadows */
  --shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px 0 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
  --shadow-md: 0 4px 6px -1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
  --shadow-lg: 0 10px 15px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
}

/* === Dark Mode Colors (Auto-detect system preference) === */
@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
  :root:not(.light-mode-forced) {
    /* Primary Colors - Lighter blues for dark bg */
    --primary-blue: #60a5fa;
    --primary-blue-dark: #3b82f6;
    --primary-blue-light: #93c5fd;
    --primary-blue-pale: #1e3a5f;
    
    /* Secondary Colors - Softer oranges */
    --secondary-orange: #fb923c;
    --secondary-orange-dark: #f97316;
    --secondary-orange-light: #fdba74;
    --secondary-orange-pale: #3a2817;
    
    /* Tertiary Colors */
    --tertiary-purple: #a78bfa;
    --tertiary-purple-dark: #8b5cf6;
    --tertiary-purple-light: #c4b5fd;
    
    /* Neutral Colors */
    --text-primary: #e5e7eb;
    --text-secondary: #d1d5db;
    --text-tertiary: #9ca3af;
    --bg-primary: #0f172a;
    --bg-secondary: #1e293b;
    --bg-tertiary: #334155;
    --border-color: #475569;
    --border-color-light: #334155;
    
    /* Semantic Colors */
    --success-color: #22d3ee; /* Bright cyan */
    --warning-color: #fb923c; /* Bright orange */
    --error-color: #ef4444; /* Bright red */
    --info-color: #60a5fa; /* Bright blue */
    
    /* Graph Colors (Brightened for dark background) */
    --graph-color-1: #60a5fa; /* Bright Blue */
    --graph-color-2: #fb923c; /* Bright Orange */
    --graph-color-3: #a78bfa; /* Bright Purple */
    --graph-color-4: #22d3ee; /* Bright Cyan */
    --graph-color-5: #f97316; /* Bright Deep Orange */
    --graph-color-6: #94a3b8; /* Light Slate */
    
    /* Interactive Elements */
    --link-color: #60a5fa;
    --link-hover: #93c5fd;
    --focus-outline: #3b82f6;
    
    /* Shadows (more prominent in dark mode) */
    --shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px 0 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3);
    --shadow-md: 0 4px 6px -1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4);
    --shadow-lg: 0 10px 15px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  }
}

/* Manual Dark Mode Override */
body.dark-mode {
  /* Primary Colors */
  --primary-blue: #60a5fa;
  --primary-blue-dark: #3b82f6;
  --primary-blue-light: #93c5fd;
  --primary-blue-pale: #1e3a5f;
  
  /* Secondary Colors */
  --secondary-orange: #fb923c;
  --secondary-orange-dark: #f97316;
  --secondary-orange-light: #fdba74;
  --secondary-orange-pale: #3a2817;
  
  /* Tertiary Colors */
  --tertiary-purple: #a78bfa;
  --tertiary-purple-dark: #8b5cf6;
  --tertiary-purple-light: #c4b5fd;
  
  /* Neutral Colors */
  --text-primary: #e5e7eb;
  --text-secondary: #d1d5db;
  --text-tertiary: #9ca3af;
  --bg-primary: #0f172a;
  --bg-secondary: #1e293b;
  --bg-tertiary: #334155;
  --border-color: #475569;
  --border-color-light: #334155;
  
  /* Semantic Colors */
  --success-color: #22d3ee;
  --warning-color: #fb923c;
  --error-color: #ef4444;
  --info-color: #60a5fa;
  
  /* Graph Colors */
  --graph-color-1: #60a5fa;
  --graph-color-2: #fb923c;
  --graph-color-3: #a78bfa;
  --graph-color-4: #22d3ee;
  --graph-color-5: #f97316;
  --graph-color-6: #94a3b8;
  
  /* Interactive Elements */
  --link-color: #60a5fa;
  --link-hover: #93c5fd;
  --focus-outline: #3b82f6;
  
  /* Shadows */
  --shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px 0 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3);
  --shadow-md: 0 4px 6px -1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4);
  --shadow-lg: 0 10px 15px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
}

/* Base Styles Using CSS Variables */
body {
  background-color: var(--bg-primary);
  color: var(--text-primary);
  transition: background-color 0.3s ease, color 0.3s ease;
}

h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 {
  color: var(--text-primary);
}

a {
  color: var(--link-color);
}

a:hover {
  color: var(--link-hover);
}

/* Mobile-Friendly Responsive Design */
@media screen and (max-width: 768px) {
  body {
    font-size: 16px;
    line-height: 1.6;
    padding: 10px;
  }
  
  h1 { font-size: 1.8em; }
  h2 { font-size: 1.5em; }
  h3 { font-size: 1.3em; }
  h4 { font-size: 1.1em; }
  
  .main-container {
    max-width: 100% !important;
    padding: 0 10px;
  }
  
  /* Make tables scrollable on mobile */
  table {
    display: block;
    overflow-x: auto;
    white-space: nowrap;
    font-size: 0.9em;
  }
  
  /* Adjust TOC for mobile */
  #TOC {
    position: static !important;
    width: 100% !important;
    margin-bottom: 20px;
    border: 1px solid var(--border-color);
    border-radius: 5px;
    padding: 15px;
    background-color: var(--bg-secondary);
  }
  
  /* Hierarchical TOC font sizes for mobile */
  .tocify-item {
    font-size: 0.9em;
  }
  
  .tocify-header {
    font-weight: 600;
    font-size: 1em;
  }
  
  /* Make code blocks scrollable */
  pre {
    overflow-x: auto;
    font-size: 0.85em;
  }
  
  /* Adjust figure sizes */
  img {
    max-width: 100%;
    height: auto;
  }
}

/* Desktop and Tablet Floating TOC */
@media screen and (min-width: 769px) {
  body {
    font-size: 16px;
    line-height: 1.7;
  }
  
  .main-container {
    max-width: 1200px;
    margin-left: auto;
    margin-right: auto;
  }
  
  #TOC {
    position: fixed !important;
    top: 60px;
    left: 20px;
    width: 300px;
    max-height: calc(100vh - 80px);
    overflow-y: auto;
    padding: 20px;
    background-color: var(--bg-secondary);
    border: 1px solid var(--border-color);
    border-radius: 8px;
    box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg);
  }
  
  /* Hierarchical TOC Styling */
  /* Level 1 (Main chapters) - Bold and largest */
  .tocify > .tocify-header > a {
    font-weight: 700 !important;
    font-size: 1.1em !important;
    padding: 8px 10px !important;
    border-left: 3px solid var(--primary-blue);
    color: var(--primary-blue) !important;
  }
  
  /* Level 2 (Major sections) */
  .tocify-subheader .tocify-item a {
    font-weight: 600 !important;
    font-size: 0.95em !important;
    padding: 6px 10px 6px 20px !important;
  }
  
  /* Level 3 (Sub-sections) */
  .tocify-subheader .tocify-subheader .tocify-item a {
    font-weight: 500 !important;
    font-size: 0.88em !important;
    padding: 5px 10px 5px 30px !important;
  }
  
  /* Level 4 (Sub-sub-sections) */
  .tocify-subheader .tocify-subheader .tocify-subheader .tocify-item a {
    font-weight: 400 !important;
    font-size: 0.82em !important;
    padding: 4px 10px 4px 40px !important;
    color: var(--text-tertiary) !important;
  }
  
  /* Active item highlighting */
  .tocify-item.active > a,
  .tocify-focus > a {
    background-color: var(--primary-blue-pale) !important;
    border-left: 3px solid var(--primary-blue);
    font-weight: 600 !important;
  }
  
  /* Hover effects for expand/collapse */
  .tocify-item > a:hover {
    background-color: var(--primary-blue-pale) !important;
    transform: translateX(2px);
  }
  
  #TOC ul {
    padding-left: 0;
  }
  
  #TOC li {
    margin: 3px 0;
    list-style-type: none;
  }
  
  #TOC a {
    color: var(--text-secondary);
    text-decoration: none;
    display: block;
    padding: 5px 8px;
    border-radius: 4px;
    transition: all 0.2s;
  }
  
  #TOC a:hover {
    background-color: var(--primary-blue-pale);
    color: var(--primary-blue);
    transform: translateX(2px);
  }
  
  #TOC .active {
    background-color: var(--primary-blue);
    color: white !important;
    font-weight: bold;
  }
  
  /* Override tocify's default icons with custom arrows */
  .tocify-icon-down:before {
    content: '\u25bc' !important;
    font-size: 0.7em;
    color: var(--text-tertiary);
    margin-right: 5px;
  }
  
  .tocify-icon-right:before {
    content: '\u25b6' !important;
    font-size: 0.7em;
    color: var(--text-tertiary);
    margin-right: 5px;
  }
  
  /* Adjust content margin for TOC */
  .row-fluid {
    margin-left: 320px;
    padding-right: 20px;
  }
}

/* Expandable Glossary Boxes */
.glossary-term {
  display: inline-block;
  position: relative;
  color: var(--primary-blue);
  font-weight: 600;
  cursor: pointer;
  border-bottom: 2px dotted var(--primary-blue);
  padding: 0 2px;
  transition: all 0.2s;
}

.glossary-term:hover {
  color: var(--link-hover);
  border-bottom-color: var(--link-hover);
  background-color: var(--primary-blue-pale);
}

.glossary-term::after {
  content: ' ⓘ';
  font-size: 0.8em;
  color: var(--primary-blue);
  vertical-align: super;
}

.glossary-content {
  display: none;
  position: relative;
  background-color: var(--primary-blue-pale);
  border-left: 4px solid var(--primary-blue);
  padding: 15px 20px;
  margin: 10px 0;
  border-radius: 4px;
  box-shadow: var(--shadow-md);
  animation: slideDown 0.3s ease-out;
}

.glossary-content.active {
  display: block;
}

.glossary-content h4 {
  margin-top: 0;
  color: var(--primary-blue);
  font-size: 1.1em;
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-color);
  padding-bottom: 8px;
  margin-bottom: 12px;
}

.glossary-content p {
  margin: 8px 0;
  line-height: 1.6;
}

.glossary-content .close-glossary {
  float: right;
  cursor: pointer;
  color: var(--text-tertiary);
  font-size: 1.2em;
  font-weight: bold;
  line-height: 1;
  padding: 0 8px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  transition: all 0.2s;
}

.glossary-content .close-glossary:hover {
  background-color: var(--accent-orange-pale);
  color: var(--accent-red);
}

@keyframes slideDown {
  from {
    opacity: 0;
    max-height: 0;
    padding-top: 0;
    padding-bottom: 0;
  }
  to {
    opacity: 1;
    max-height: 500px;
    padding-top: 15px;
    padding-bottom: 15px;
  }
}

/* Bibliography Interactive Styling (similar to glossary) */
.reference-item {
  cursor: pointer;
  color: var(--accent-orange);
  text-decoration: none;
  transition: all 0.2s;
  display: inline-block;
  padding: 2px 6px;
  border-radius: 3px;
  margin: 2px 0;
}

.reference-item:hover {
  background-color: var(--accent-orange-pale);
  color: var(--link-hover);
}

.reference-item::after {
  content: ' 📖';
  font-size: 0.9em;
}

.reference-content {
  display: none;
  position: relative;
  background-color: var(--accent-orange-pale);
  border-left: 4px solid var(--accent-orange);
  padding: 15px 20px;
  margin: 10px 0;
  border-radius: 4px;
  box-shadow: var(--shadow-md);
  animation: slideDown 0.3s ease-out;
}

.reference-content.active {
  display: block;
}

.reference-content h5 {
  margin-top: 0;
  color: var(--accent-orange);
  font-size: 1em;
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-color);
  padding-bottom: 6px;
  margin-bottom: 10px;
}

.reference-content .close-reference {
  float: right;
  cursor: pointer;
  color: var(--text-tertiary);
  font-size: 1.2em;
  font-weight: bold;
  line-height: 1;
  padding: 0 8px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  transition: all 0.2s;
}

.reference-content .close-reference:hover {
  background-color: var(--accent-orange-pale);
  color: var(--accent-red);
}

/* General Improvements */
body {
  font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif;
  color: var(--text-primary);
  background-color: var(--bg-primary);
}

h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 {
  color: var(--text-primary);
  margin-top: 1.5em;
  margin-bottom: 0.5em;
  font-weight: 600;
}

h1 {
  border-bottom: 3px solid var(--primary-blue);
  padding-bottom: 10px;
  margin-top: 0;
}

h2 {
  border-bottom: 2px solid var(--border-color);
  padding-bottom: 8px;
}

/* Tabset Styling */
.nav-tabs {
  border-bottom: 2px solid var(--primary-blue);
  margin-bottom: 20px;
}

.nav-tabs > li > a {
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  border-radius: 4px 4px 0 0;
  padding: 10px 20px;
}

.nav-tabs > li.active > a {
  background-color: var(--primary-blue);
  color: white;
  border-color: var(--primary-blue);
}

/* Table Styling */
table {
  border-collapse: collapse;
  width: 100%;
  margin: 20px 0;
  box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm);
}

table thead {
  background-color: var(--primary-blue);
  color: white;
}

table th, table td {
  padding: 12px 15px;
  text-align: left;
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-color);
}

table tbody tr:hover {
  background-color: var(--primary-blue-pale);
}

/* Code Blocks */
pre {
  background-color: var(--bg-secondary);
  border: 1px solid var(--border-color);
  border-radius: 5px;
  padding: 15px;
  overflow-x: auto;
}

code {
  background-color: var(--bg-secondary);
  padding: 2px 6px;
  border-radius: 3px;
  font-family: "Courier New", monospace;
}

/* Links */
a {
  color: var(--link-color);
  text-decoration: none;
}

a:hover {
  color: var(--link-hover);
  text-decoration: underline;
}

/* Blockquotes */
blockquote {
  border-left: 4px solid var(--primary-blue);
  padding-left: 20px;
  margin-left: 0;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  font-style: italic;
  background-color: var(--bg-secondary);
  padding: 10px 20px;
  border-radius: 0 5px 5px 0;
}

/* Print Styles */
@media print {
  #TOC {
    display: none;
  }
  
  .main-container {
    max-width: 100%;
    margin: 0;
  }
  
  .nav-tabs {
    display: none;
  }
  
  .tab-content > .tab-pane {
    display: block !important;
    opacity: 1 !important;
  }
}

/* Smooth Scrolling */
html {
  scroll-behavior: smooth;
}

/* Back to Top Button (optional enhancement) */
.back-to-top {
  position: fixed;
  bottom: 20px;
  right: 20px;
  background-color: var(--primary-blue);
  color: white;
  padding: 10px 15px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  display: none;
  z-index: 1000;
  cursor: pointer;
  box-shadow: var(--shadow-md);
}

.back-to-top:hover {
  background-color: var(--link-hover);
}

/* ===== DARK MODE / NIGHT VIEW ===== */

/* Toggle Button Styling */
.theme-toggle {
  position: fixed;
  top: 20px;
  right: 20px;
  z-index: 9999;
  background-color: var(--primary-blue);
  color: white;
  border: none;
  padding: 12px 18px;
  border-radius: 25px;
  cursor: pointer;
  font-size: 14px;
  font-weight: 600;
  box-shadow: var(--shadow-md);
  transition: all 0.3s ease;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 8px;
}

.theme-toggle:hover {
  background-color: var(--link-hover);
  transform: translateY(-2px);
  box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg);
}

.theme-toggle:active {
  transform: translateY(0);
}

.theme-icon {
  font-size: 18px;
}

/* Dark Mode Styles - Now handled by CSS Variables defined at top of file */
/* The CSS variables at the beginning of this file define all colors for both
   light and dark modes. Dark mode is automatically applied via:
   1. @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) - auto-detects system preference
   2. body.dark-mode class - manual toggle override */

/* Smooth transition for theme changes */
body,
#TOC,
table,
pre,
code,
.glossary-content,
.reference-content,
details,
details summary {
  transition: background-color 0.3s ease, color 0.3s ease, border-color 0.3s ease;
}
</style>

<script>
// Tab Navigation for RMarkdown Tabbed Content
(function() {
  'use strict';
  
  console.log('Tab Navigation Script Loaded');
  
  // Helper: Escape CSS selector for IDs that start with numbers
  function escapeSelector(id) {
    // If id starts with a number, escape it
    if (id && id.match(/^[0-9]/)) {
      return '#\\3' + id.charAt(0) + ' ' + id.slice(1);
    }
    return id;
  }
  
  // VERSION: 2026-02-01-v3 - Fixed tocify selector
  console.log('🔵 JavaScript Version: 2026-02-01-v3 - Tab Navigation Enhanced');
  
  // Wait for tocify to build the TOC
  function waitForReady(callback) {
    var checkCount = 0;
    var maxChecks = 150; // Extended timeout
    var checkInterval = setInterval(function() {
      // Check if tocify has populated the TOC - try multiple selectors
      var tocLinks = document.querySelectorAll('.tocify-item a, .tocify a[href^="#"], #TOC a[href^="#"]');
      checkCount++;
      
      if (checkCount % 10 === 0) {
        console.log('Waiting for TOC... check', checkCount, '- found', tocLinks.length, 'links');
        console.log('  - Checking selectors: .tocify-item, .tocify a, #TOC a');
      }
      
      if (tocLinks.length > 0) {
        clearInterval(checkInterval);
        console.log('✅ TOC ready with', tocLinks.length, 'links');
        callback();
      }
      
      if (checkCount >= maxChecks) {
        clearInterval(checkInterval);
        console.log('⚠️ Timeout after', checkCount, 'checks - found', tocLinks.length, 'links');
        // Log what's in the TOC container
        var tocContainer = document.getElementById('TOC');
        if (tocContainer) {
          console.log('TOC container exists, innerHTML length:', tocContainer.innerHTML.length);
          console.log('TOC container classes:', tocContainer.className);
        }
        callback();
      }
    }, 100);
  }
  
  function initTabNavigation() {
    console.log('Initializing tab navigation for tocify TOC...');
    
    // Get all TOC links - use multiple selectors to find them
    var tocLinks = document.querySelectorAll('.tocify-item a, .tocify a[href^="#"], #TOC a[href^="#"]');
    console.log('Found ' + tocLinks.length + ' TOC links');
    
    // Helper: Find parent with class
    function findParentWithClass(element, className) {
      var current = element;
      var depth = 0;
      while (current && current !== document.body && depth < 20) {
        if (current.classList && current.classList.contains(className)) {
          return current;
        }
        current = current.parentElement;
        depth++;
      }
      return null;
    }
    
    // Helper: Activate tab containing element
    function activateTabForElement(element) {
      console.log('Attempting to activate tab for element:', element.id || element.tagName);
      
      var tabPane = findParentWithClass(element, 'tab-pane');
      
      if (!tabPane) {
        console.log('Element is not in a tab-pane');
        return false;
      }
      
      console.log('Found tab-pane:', tabPane.id);
      
      if (tabPane.classList.contains('active')) {
        console.log('Tab already active');
        return false;
      }
      
      var tabPaneId = tabPane.getAttribute('id');
      if (!tabPaneId) {
        console.log('Tab pane has no ID');
        return false;
      }
      
      // Find the tab link - try multiple strategies
      var tabLink = null;
      
      // Strategy 1: Look for data-toggle="tab"
      var selector = 'a[href="#' + tabPaneId + '"][data-toggle="tab"]';
      tabLink = document.querySelector(selector);
      console.log('Strategy 1 (data-toggle):', tabLink ? 'Found' : 'Not found');
      
      // Strategy 2: Look in nav-tabs/nav-pills
      if (!tabLink) {
        selector = 'a[href="#' + tabPaneId + '"]';
        var allLinks = document.querySelectorAll(selector);
        console.log('Strategy 2: Found', allLinks.length, 'links matching href');
        
        for (var i = 0; i < allLinks.length; i++) {
          var link = allLinks[i];
          var nav = findParentWithClass(link, 'nav-tabs') || findParentWithClass(link, 'nav-pills');
          if (nav) {
            tabLink = link;
            console.log('Found tab link in nav');
            break;
          }
        }
      }
      
      if (!tabLink) {
        console.log('Could not find tab link for:', tabPaneId);
        return false;
      }
      
      console.log('Activating tab link:', tabLink.textContent.trim());
      
      // Activate tab using jQuery/Bootstrap
      if (typeof $ !== 'undefined' && $.fn && $.fn.tab) {
        $(tabLink).tab('show');
        console.log('Tab activated successfully via jQuery');
        return true;
      } else {
        console.log('jQuery/Bootstrap tab not available');
      }
      
      return false;
    }
    
    // Add click handlers to all TOC links - use capture phase to run before tocify
    tocLinks.forEach(function(link) {
      link.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
        var href = this.getAttribute('href');
        if (!href || href === '#') return;
        
        console.log('=== TOC Link Clicked ===');
        console.log('Link text:', this.textContent.trim());
        console.log('Target href:', href);
        
        // Small delay to let tocify handle its own logic first
        setTimeout(function() {
          var targetSelector = escapeSelector(href);
          var target;
          
          try {
            target = document.querySelector(targetSelector);
          } catch(e) {
            console.log('Invalid selector:', targetSelector, '- trying getElementById');
            // Try using getElementById instead
            var id = href.substring(1);
            target = document.getElementById(id);
          }
          
          if (target) {
            console.log('Target element found:', target.tagName, target.id);
            
            // Activate tab if needed
            var tabActivated = activateTabForElement(target);
            
            // Scroll to target with delay if tab was activated
            setTimeout(function() {
              console.log('Scrolling to target');
              target.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' });
            }, tabActivated ? 300 : 50);
          } else {
            console.log('Target element not found for:', href);
          }
        }, 50);
      }, true); // Use capture phase
    });
    
    // Handle page load with hash
    if (window.location.hash) {
      console.log('Page loaded with hash:', window.location.hash);
      setTimeout(function() {
        var targetSelector = escapeSelector(window.location.hash);
        var target;
        
        try {
          target = document.querySelector(targetSelector);
        } catch(e) {
          console.log('Invalid hash selector, using getElementById');
          var id = window.location.hash.substring(1);
          target = document.getElementById(id);
        }
        
        if (target) {
          activateTabForElement(target);
          target.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' });
        }
      }, 1000); // Longer delay for page load
    }
    
    console.log('Tab navigation initialized');
  }
  
  // Initialize when ready
  if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
      waitForReady(initTabNavigation);
    });
  } else {
    waitForReady(initTabNavigation);
  }
})();

// Glossary term toggle functionality
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  // Add click handlers to all glossary terms
  var glossaryTerms = document.querySelectorAll('.glossary-term');
  
  glossaryTerms.forEach(function(term) {
    term.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
      e.preventDefault();
      var termId = this.getAttribute('data-term');
      var content = document.getElementById(termId);
      
      if (content) {
        // Toggle active class
        content.classList.toggle('active');
        
        // Close other open glossary boxes
        document.querySelectorAll('.glossary-content.active').forEach(function(otherContent) {
          if (otherContent !== content) {
            otherContent.classList.remove('active');
          }
        });
        
        // Scroll to content if opened
        if (content.classList.contains('active')) {
          setTimeout(function() {
            content.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'nearest' });
          }, 100);
        }
      }
    });
  });
  
  // Add click handlers to close buttons
  document.querySelectorAll('.close-glossary').forEach(function(closeBtn) {
    closeBtn.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
      e.stopPropagation();
      this.closest('.glossary-content').classList.remove('active');
    });
  });
  
  // Close glossary boxes when clicking outside
  document.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
    if (!e.target.closest('.glossary-term') && !e.target.closest('.glossary-content')) {
      document.querySelectorAll('.glossary-content.active').forEach(function(content) {
        content.classList.remove('active');
      });
    }
  });
});

// Bibliography reference toggle functionality (similar to glossary)
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  // Add click handlers to all bibliography reference items
  var referenceItems = document.querySelectorAll('.reference-item');
  
  referenceItems.forEach(function(item) {
    item.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
      e.preventDefault();
      var refId = this.getAttribute('data-ref');
      var content = document.getElementById(refId);
      
      if (content) {
        // Toggle active class
        content.classList.toggle('active');
        
        // Close other open reference boxes
        document.querySelectorAll('.reference-content.active').forEach(function(otherContent) {
          if (otherContent !== content) {
            otherContent.classList.remove('active');
          }
        });
        
        // Scroll to content if opened
        if (content.classList.contains('active')) {
          setTimeout(function() {
            content.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'nearest' });
          }, 100);
        }
      }
    });
  });
  
  // Add click handlers to close buttons
  document.querySelectorAll('.close-reference').forEach(function(closeBtn) {
    closeBtn.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
      e.stopPropagation();
      this.closest('.reference-content').classList.remove('active');
    });
  });
  
  // Close reference boxes when clicking outside
  document.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
    if (!e.target.closest('.reference-item') && !e.target.closest('.reference-content')) {
      document.querySelectorAll('.reference-content.active').forEach(function(content) {
        content.classList.remove('active');
      });
    }
  });
});
</script>

<!-- Night Mode Toggle Button -->
<button class="theme-toggle" id="theme-toggle" aria-label="Toggle dark mode">
  <span class="theme-icon" id="theme-icon">🌙</span>
  <span id="theme-text">Night View</span>
</button>

<script>
// Dark Mode / Night View Toggle with Auto-Detection
(function() {
  'use strict';
  
  const themeToggle = document.getElementById('theme-toggle');
  const themeIcon = document.getElementById('theme-icon');
  const themeText = document.getElementById('theme-text');
  const body = document.body;
  
  // Function to apply theme
  function applyTheme(theme) {
    if (theme === 'dark') {
      body.classList.add('dark-mode');
      themeIcon.textContent = '☀️';
      themeText.textContent = 'Day View';
    } else {
      body.classList.remove('dark-mode');
      themeIcon.textContent = '🌙';
      themeText.textContent = 'Night View';
    }
  }
  
  // Check for saved theme preference or auto-detect from system
  const savedTheme = localStorage.getItem('theme');
  let currentTheme;
  
  if (savedTheme) {
    // User has a saved preference - use it
    currentTheme = savedTheme;
    console.log('📱 Using saved theme preference:', currentTheme);
  } else {
    // No saved preference - auto-detect from system (prefers-color-scheme)
    const prefersDark = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches;
    currentTheme = prefersDark ? 'dark' : 'light';
    console.log('🌓 Auto-detected system theme preference:', currentTheme);
  }
  
  // Apply the determined theme on page load
  applyTheme(currentTheme);
  
  // Listen for system theme changes (if user changes OS settings)
  window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').addEventListener('change', function(e) {
    // Only auto-update if user hasn't set a manual preference
    if (!localStorage.getItem('theme')) {
      const newTheme = e.matches ? 'dark' : 'light';
      console.log('🌓 System theme changed to:', newTheme);
      applyTheme(newTheme);
    }
  });
  
  // Toggle theme on button click (manual override)
  themeToggle.addEventListener('click', function() {
    body.classList.toggle('dark-mode');
    
    let theme = 'light';
    if (body.classList.contains('dark-mode')) {
      theme = 'dark';
      themeIcon.textContent = '☀️';
      themeText.textContent = 'Day View';
    } else {
      themeIcon.textContent = '🌙';
      themeText.textContent = 'Night View';
    }
    
    // Save theme preference (manual override)
    localStorage.setItem('theme', theme);
    console.log('👤 Manual theme override to:', theme);
  });
  
  // Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl+Shift+D to toggle dark mode
  document.addEventListener('keydown', function(e) {
    if (e.ctrlKey && e.shiftKey && e.key === 'D') {
      e.preventDefault();
      themeToggle.click();
    }
  });
  
  // Double-click the button to clear saved preference and revert to auto-detection
  themeToggle.addEventListener('dblclick', function(e) {
    e.preventDefault();
    localStorage.removeItem('theme');
    const prefersDark = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches;
    const autoTheme = prefersDark ? 'dark' : 'light';
    applyTheme(autoTheme);
    console.log('🔄 Reset to auto-detect mode. Theme:', autoTheme);
    
    // Show brief notification
    const notification = document.createElement('div');
    notification.textContent = 'Auto-detect mode enabled';
    notification.style.cssText = 'position: fixed; top: 70px; right: 20px; background: var(--primary-blue); color: white; padding: 10px 15px; border-radius: 5px; z-index: 10000; font-size: 14px;';
    document.body.appendChild(notification);
    setTimeout(() => notification.remove(), 2000);
  });
})();
</script>

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.align = 'center',
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 7,
  cache = FALSE
)

# Load required libraries
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(viridis)
library(gridExtra)

# Set plot theme
theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 12))
```

---

# Introduction

## Overview

This chapter provides a comprehensive foundation in GaN (Gallium Nitride) transistor technology for RF power amplifier (PA) design, with extensive evidence drawn from the **IFX Tx_Baseline project (2022-2023)**. The chapter bridges theoretical principles with practical implementation, documenting the systematic transistor downselection process that guided real-world PA module development.

### Chapter Objectives

1. **Establish GaN Technology Foundation**
   - Understand GaN HEMT device physics and operating principles
   - Compare GaN advantages versus legacy technologies (LDMOS, GaAs)
   - Connect material properties to RF performance characteristics

2. **Master RF Characterization Methods**
   - Small-signal S-parameter measurements and interpretation
   - Large-signal load-pull optimization techniques
   - Linearity and efficiency trade-off analysis

3. **Document Systematic Downselection**
   - Learn Design of Experiments (DOE) methodology
   - Analyze 9+ transistor candidates across multiple technologies
   - Understand weighted selection criteria for real-world applications

4. **Apply Practical Design Knowledge**
   - Biasing strategies for linearity versus efficiency optimization
   - Matching network topologies and design procedures
   - Stability analysis and thermal management considerations

### Context: IFX Tx_Baseline Project

**Project Scope:**
- **Timeframe**: 2022-2023
- **Objective**: Develop optimized 5G NR power amplifier module (PAM_B)
- **Application**: Infrastructure base station transmitter
- **Frequency Band**: 3.3-3.8 GHz (5G n77/n78)
- **Systematic Approach**: 17+ DOE builds exploring device size, technology, bias, and matching

**Key Artifacts Referenced:**
- 80+ technical documents including specifications, design reviews, measurements, and validation reports
- 9 DOE simulation builds with systematic parameter variation
- Comprehensive measurement data validating design methodology
- Final baseline results demonstrating specification compliance

This chapter transforms project-specific learnings into generalizable PA design principles, providing readers with both theoretical understanding and evidence-based practical guidance.

---

## Learning Outcomes

Upon completing this chapter, you will be able to:

1. **Explain GaN Device Physics**
   - Describe AlGaN/GaN HEMT structure and 2DEG formation
   - Interpret DC I-V characteristics and key operating parameters
   - Relate material properties to RF performance advantages

2. **Perform RF Characterization**
   - Conduct S-parameter measurements and extract stability factors
   - Execute load-pull measurements to optimize impedances
   - Measure and interpret linearity metrics (IM3, ACPR, EVM)

3. **Execute Transistor Downselection**
   - Design systematic DOE exploration plans
   - Apply weighted selection criteria balancing multiple metrics
   - Document trade-off analysis and selection rationale

4. **Design Practical PA Circuits**
   - Calculate optimal bias points for target operating class
   - Design input/output matching networks with harmonic control
   - Ensure unconditional stability across all frequencies
   - Implement thermal management strategies

5. **Validate with Simulation**
   - Build transistor models from measurement data
   - Correlate simulated versus measured performance
   - Predict PA behavior across operating conditions

---

## Roadmap

### Section Organization

```{r roadmap-diagram, fig.cap="Chapter 1 Section Flow and Dependencies", out.width="100%"}
# Placeholder for section dependency diagram
# Will create visual flowchart showing:
# 1.1 Introduction → 1.2 Device Physics → 1.3 Characterization →
# 1.4 Downselection → 1.5 Design → 1.6 Modeling → 1.7 Reliability → 1.8 Summary
```

**Section 1.1 (Introduction)**: Context and motivation for GaN technology adoption in modern RF systems.

**Section 1.2 (GaN Device Physics)**: Fundamental operating principles of AlGaN/GaN HEMTs, 2DEG formation, and DC characteristics.

**Section 1.3 (RF Characterization)**: Small-signal and large-signal measurement techniques essential for PA design.

**Section 1.4 (Downselection Process)**: ⭐ **CORE SECTION** - Systematic DOE methodology with IFX Tx_Baseline evidence demonstrating how to select optimal transistors from multiple candidates.

**Section 1.5 (Practical Design)**: Bias networks, matching topologies, stability analysis, and layout considerations.

**Section 1.6 (Modeling & Simulation)**: Transistor model extraction, validation, and simulation tool usage.

**Section 1.7 (Reliability & Packaging)**: Thermal management, reliability screening, and package selection (Minipac V2).

**Section 1.8 (Summary)**: Key takeaways, best practices checklist, and transition to Chapter 2 (Multi-Stage PA Design).

---

## Prerequisites

### Assumed Knowledge

Readers should have familiarity with:

- **Electrical Engineering Fundamentals**:
  - AC/DC circuit analysis
  - Impedance matching concepts
  - S-parameters and Smith charts
  - Basic semiconductor physics

- **RF/Microwave Basics**:
  - Transmission line theory
  - Distributed elements (microstrip, stripline)
  - Gain, efficiency, and linearity definitions
  - Frequency-domain measurements (VNA usage)

- **Simulation Tools** (helpful but not required):
  - ADS (Advanced Design System) or similar
  - Load-pull simulation capability
  - Linear/nonlinear analysis methods

### Recommended Background Reading

For readers needing refreshers:

1. **RF/Microwave Circuits**:
   - Pozar, "Microwave Engineering" [@pozar2011] (Chapters 2, 4, 12)
   - Gonzalez, "Microwave Transistor Amplifiers" [@gonzalez1997]

2. **GaN Device Physics**:
   - Mishra & Shen, "Nitride Semiconductor Devices" [@mishra2002]
   - Pengelly et al., "A Review of GaN on SiC HEMTs" [@pengelly2012]

3. **Power Amplifier Design**:
   - Cripps, "RF Power Amplifiers for Wireless Communications" [@cripps2006]
   - Grebennikov, "RF and Microwave Power Amplifier Design" [@grebennikov2015]
   - Colantonio et al., "High Efficiency RF and Microwave Solid State Power Amplifiers" [@colantonio2009]

---

# GaN Device Physics

## AlGaN/GaN HEMT Structure

### Device Cross-Section

The AlGaN/GaN High Electron Mobility Transistor (HEMT) exploits <span class="glossary-term" data-term="glossary-heterojunction">**heterojunction engineering**</span> to achieve superior RF performance [@mishra2002; @pengelly2012]. The fundamental structure consists of:

<div id="glossary-heterojunction" class="glossary-content">
  <span class="close-glossary">×</span>
  <h4>📖 Heterojunction Engineering</h4>
  <p><strong>Definition:</strong> The practice of joining two different semiconductor materials with different bandgaps to create unique electrical properties at their interface.</p>
  <p><strong>In GaN HEMTs:</strong> AlGaN (wider bandgap, ~4.0 eV) is grown on GaN (narrower bandgap, 3.4 eV), creating a conduction band discontinuity that forms a quantum well where the 2DEG accumulates.</p>
  <p><strong>Why It Matters:</strong> This interface engineering creates high-density, high-mobility electron channels without requiring doping, resulting in superior RF performance compared to conventional MOSFETs.</p>
</div>

```{r hemt-structure-diagram, fig.cap="AlGaN/GaN HEMT Cross-Sectional Structure with 2DEG Formation [@mishra2002]", out.width="85%"}
# Placeholder for device structure diagram
# Will create visual showing:
# - Gate, Source, Drain terminals
# - AlGaN barrier layer (~25nm)
# - GaN channel layer with 2DEG indicated
# - Buffer layers (AlN, AlGaN nucleation)
# - Substrate (SiC or Si)
# - Passivation layer
# - Field plate (if present)
```

**Key Layers:**

1. **Gate Electrode**: Schottky contact controlling channel conductance
2. **AlGaN Barrier Layer** (~20-30 nm):
   - Higher bandgap (3.8-4.2 eV) than GaN
   - Creates conduction band discontinuity
   - Induces piezoelectric and spontaneous polarization [@ibbetson2000]
3. **GaN Channel Layer** (~1-2 μm):
   - Wide bandgap (3.4 eV) [@sze2006]
   - Location of 2DEG formation
   - Undoped or lightly doped
4. **Buffer Layers**:
   - Transition layers (AlGaN grading, AlN nucleation)
   - Isolate channel from substrate
   - Suppress short-channel effects
5. **Substrate**:
   - SiC (Silicon Carbide) - Best thermal conductivity
   - Si (Silicon) - Cost-effective, larger wafers
   - GaN - Lattice-matched, premium performance

### <span class="glossary-term" data-term="glossary-2deg">Two-Dimensional Electron Gas (2DEG)</span>

<div id="glossary-2deg" class="glossary-content">
  <span class="close-glossary">×</span>
  <h4>📖 Two-Dimensional Electron Gas (2DEG)</h4>
  <p><strong>Definition:</strong> A quantum-mechanical phenomenon where electrons are confined to move freely in only two dimensions (along the interface) but are tightly confined in the third dimension (perpendicular to interface).</p>
  <p><strong>How It Forms in GaN:</strong> At the AlGaN/GaN heterointerface, the combination of polarization-induced electric fields and conduction band offset creates a triangular potential well. Electrons accumulate in this well, forming a sheet of charge just a few nanometers thick.</p>
  <p><strong>Key Properties:</strong></p>
  <ul>
    <li>High sheet density: n<sub>s</sub> = 1-2 × 10<sup>13</sup> cm<sup>-2</sup> (10× higher than GaAs)</li>
    <li>High mobility: μ = 1500-2000 cm²/V·s at room temperature</li>
    <li>No ionized impurity scattering (undoped channel)</li>
  </ul>
  <p><strong>Why It Matters for RF:</strong> The 2DEG enables very high current densities with low resistance, critical for high-power RF amplification.</p>
</div>

**Formation Mechanism:**

The 2DEG is a **quantum well** of high-mobility electrons confined at the AlGaN/GaN interface. It forms due to:

$$
n_s = \frac{\varepsilon_0 \varepsilon_r E_{piezo}}{q} + \frac{\Delta E_C}{q d}
$$

**Variable Definitions**:

- $n_s$ [cm⁻²] = Two-dimensional electron gas (2DEG) sheet density, number of electrons per unit area at interface
- $\varepsilon_0$ [F/m] = Permittivity of free space = 8.854 × 10⁻¹² F/m (fundamental constant)
- $\varepsilon_r$ [dimensionless] = Relative permittivity of AlGaN barrier layer (typically 9-10)
- $E_{piezo}$ [V/cm] = Piezoelectric polarization-induced electric field from strain (lattice mismatch between AlGaN and GaN)
- $q$ [C] = Elementary charge = 1.602 × 10⁻¹⁹ C (fundamental constant)
- $\Delta E_C$ [eV] = Conduction band offset at AlGaN/GaN heterointerface (typically 0.3-0.5 eV)
- $d$ [nm] = AlGaN barrier layer thickness (typically 15-30 nm)

**Physical Interpretation**:

This equation has **two terms** representing different physical mechanisms:

1. **First term** $\frac{\varepsilon_0 \varepsilon_r E_{piezo}}{q}$: 
   - Electrons induced by <span class="glossary-term" data-term="glossary-piezo">**piezoelectric polarization**</span>
   - Strain from lattice mismatch (AlGaN has smaller lattice constant than GaN) creates electric field
   - This field pulls electrons to interface
   - **Dominant contribution** (~70-80% of total $n_s$)

<div id="glossary-piezo" class="glossary-content">
  <span class="close-glossary">×</span>
  <h4>📖 Piezoelectric Polarization</h4>
  <p><strong>Definition:</strong> Electric polarization (charge separation) that occurs in certain crystals when mechanical stress/strain is applied, or vice versa.</p>
  <p><strong>In GaN HEMTs:</strong> The lattice mismatch between AlGaN and GaN layers creates tensile/compressive strain. This strain induces strong electric fields (10⁶-10⁷ V/cm) normal to the interface, attracting electrons from the AlGaN to form the 2DEG.</p>
  <p><strong>Key Point:</strong> GaN (wurtzite structure) has exceptionally strong piezoelectric coefficients (~5-10× larger than GaAs), making this the dominant mechanism for 2DEG formation.</p>
  <p><strong>Design Control:</strong> Al composition in Al<sub>x</sub>Ga<sub>1-x</sub>N (typically x=0.2-0.3) directly controls strain → polarization → 2DEG density.</p>
</div>

2. **Second term** $\frac{\Delta E_C}{q d}$:
   - Electrons from **spontaneous polarization** and band offset
   - Wurtzite crystal structure of GaN has built-in polarization
   - Conduction band discontinuity creates quantum well
   - **Secondary contribution** (~20-30% of total $n_s$)

**Design Implications**:
- Higher Al content in AlGaN → more strain → higher $E_{piezo}$ → higher $n_s$ → more current
- Thinner barrier $d$ → higher field → higher $n_s$, but increases gate leakage
- Typical result: $n_s$ = 1-2 × 10¹³ cm⁻² (10× higher than GaAs HEMTs)

```{r gan-2deg-band-diagram, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Figure 1.1: GaN HEMT Structure - Band Diagram, Cross-Section, Top View, and Schematic Symbol", fig.width=12, fig.height=14}
# Enhanced visualization with 4 views
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)

# ========== BAND DIAGRAM (Top) ==========
position <- seq(-10, 50, 0.5)
AlGaN_start <- 0
AlGaN_end <- 25
GaN_start <- 25

Ec <- ifelse(position < AlGaN_start, 1.0,
       ifelse(position < AlGaN_end, 1.5 - 0.02*(position - AlGaN_start),
              0.5 + 0.15*exp(-(position - GaN_start)/3)))
Ev <- Ec - ifelse(position < AlGaN_end, 4.0, 3.4)
Ef <- 1.2
DEG_2D_density <- 0.8 * exp(-((position - GaN_start)/2)^2)

band_data <- data.frame(Position_nm = position, Ec = Ec, Ev = Ev, Ef = Ef, Density = DEG_2D_density)

p1 <- ggplot(band_data, aes(x = Position_nm)) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = -10, xmax = AlGaN_start, ymin = -4, ymax = 3,
           fill = "gray80", alpha = 0.3, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = AlGaN_start, xmax = AlGaN_end, ymin = -4, ymax = 3,
           fill = "#BBDEFB", alpha = 0.4, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = AlGaN_end, xmax = 50, ymin = -4, ymax = 3,
           fill = "#C8E6C9", alpha = 0.4, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Ec), linewidth = 1.5, color = "#E41A1C") +
  geom_line(aes(y = Ev), linewidth = 1.5, color = "#377EB8") +
  geom_line(aes(y = Ef), linewidth = 1.2, color = "#FF7F00", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_area(aes(y = -3 + Density * 1.5), fill = "#4CAF50", alpha = 0.6) +
  annotate("text", x = -5, y = 2.5, label = "Gate\nMetal", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 12, y = 2.5, label = "AlGaN\nBarrier", size = 3.5, 
           fontface = "bold", color = "#1976D2") +
  annotate("text", x = 38, y = 2.5, label = "GaN\nChannel", size = 3.5, 
           fontface = "bold", color = "#388E3C") +
  annotate("text", x = 45, y = 0.7, label = "Ec", size = 3, color = "#E41A1C", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 45, y = -2.8, label = "Ev", size = 3, color = "#377EB8", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("segment", x = AlGaN_end + 0.5, xend = AlGaN_end + 0.5, 
           y = 1.5, yend = 0.65, arrow = arrow(ends = "both", length = unit(0.15, "cm")),
           color = "#D32F2F", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = AlGaN_end + 3.5, y = 1.1, label = "ΔEc~0.4eV", 
           size = 3, color = "#D32F2F", fontface = "bold") +
  labs(title = "Band Diagram: 2DEG Formation", x = "Position (nm)", y = "Energy (eV)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5), panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

# ========== CROSS-SECTION (Middle-Top) ==========
layer_data <- data.frame(
  Layer = c("Source", "Gate", "Drain", "Passivation", "AlGaN", "2DEG", "GaN", "Buffer", "Substrate"),
  ymin = c(5, 5, 5, 4.5, 4, 3.95, 2, 1, 0),
  ymax = c(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 5, 4.5, 4, 4, 2, 1),
  xmin = c(0.5, 2, 3.5, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0),
  xmax = c(1, 3, 4, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5),
  color = c("#757575", "#757575", "#757575", "#E1BEE7", "#2196F3", "#4CAF50", "#8BC34A", "#CDDC39", "#FFC107"),
  stroke_color = c("black", "black", "black", "#9C27B0", "#0D47A1", "#1B5E20", "#558B2F", "#9E9D24", "#F57F17")
)

p2 <- ggplot(layer_data[4:9,]) +
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = color),
            color = layer_data$stroke_color[4:9], linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_rect(data = layer_data[1:3,], 
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
            fill = "#424242", color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_text(data = layer_data[1:3,], 
            aes(x = (xmin + xmax)/2, y = ymax + 0.12, label = Layer),
            size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(data = layer_data[4:9,], 
            aes(x = xmax + 0.4, y = (ymin + ymax)/2, label = Layer),
            hjust = 0, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("segment", x = 0.5, xend = 4.5, y = 3.975, yend = 3.975,
           color = "#1B5E20", linewidth = 2.5) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Cross-Section View") +
  xlim(-0.5, 6.5) + ylim(0, 5.8) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 11))

# ========== TOP VIEW (Middle-Bottom) ==========
p3 <- ggplot() +
  # Active area outline
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 10, ymin = 0, ymax = 4,
           fill = "#E8F5E9", color = "#2E7D32", linewidth = 1) +
  # Gate fingers (multi-finger layout)
  annotate("rect", xmin = 1, xmax = 1.4, ymin = 0.2, ymax = 3.8,
           fill = "#FFD54F", color = "#F57F00", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 2.5, xmax = 2.9, ymin = 0.2, ymax = 3.8,
           fill = "#FFD54F", color = "#F57F00", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 4, xmax = 4.4, ymin = 0.2, ymax = 3.8,
           fill = "#FFD54F", color = "#F57F00", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 5.5, xmax = 5.9, ymin = 0.2, ymax = 3.8,
           fill = "#FFD54F", color = "#F57F00", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 7, xmax = 7.4, ymin = 0.2, ymax = 3.8,
           fill = "#FFD54F", color = "#F57F00", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 8.5, xmax = 8.9, ymin = 0.2, ymax = 3.8,
           fill = "#FFD54F", color = "#F57F00", linewidth = 0.8) +
  # Source/Drain pads
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 0.6, ymin = 1.5, ymax = 2.5,
           fill = "#90A4AE", color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 9.4, xmax = 10, ymin = 1.5, ymax = 2.5,
           fill = "#90A4AE", color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  # Labels
  annotate("text", x = 0.3, y = 2, label = "S", size = 5, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
  annotate("text", x = 9.7, y = 2, label = "D", size = 5, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
  annotate("text", x = 5, y = 4.3, label = "Gate Fingers (6 × 2mm = 12mm total)", 
           size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = "#F57F00") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.3, y = 0.8, label = "Source", size = 2.5, hjust = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = 9.7, y = 0.8, label = "Drain", size = 2.5, hjust = 1) +
  # Dimension arrows
  annotate("segment", x = 1.2, xend = 2.7, y = 4.5, yend = 4.5,
           arrow = arrow(ends = "both", length = unit(0.1, "cm")), linewidth = 0.6) +
  annotate("text", x = 1.95, y = 4.7, label = "Pitch", size = 2.5, fontface = "italic") +
  labs(title = "Top View: Multi-Finger Layout") +
  xlim(-0.5, 10.5) + ylim(-0.5, 5) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 11))

# ========== SCHEMATIC SYMBOL (Bottom) ==========
p4 <- ggplot() +
  # HEMT symbol (D-G-S with typical layout)
  # Gate
  annotate("segment", x = 2, xend = 2, y = 1.5, yend = 2.5,
           linewidth = 2, color = "#424242") +
  annotate("segment", x = 2, xend = 1, y = 2, yend = 2,
           linewidth = 1.2, color = "#424242") +
  # Channel
  annotate("segment", x = 2.2, xend = 2.2, y = 1, yend = 3,
           linewidth = 1, color = "#1976D2", linetype = "dashed") +
  # Drain
  annotate("segment", x = 2.2, xend = 3.5, y = 3, yend = 3,
           linewidth = 1.2, color = "#424242") +
  annotate("segment", x = 3.5, xend = 3.5, y = 3, yend = 3.5,
           linewidth = 1.2, color = "#424242") +
  # Source
  annotate("segment", x = 2.2, xend = 3.5, y = 1, yend = 1,
           linewidth = 1.2, color = "#424242") +
  annotate("segment", x = 3.5, xend = 3.5, y = 1, yend = 0.5,
           linewidth = 1.2, color = "#424242") +
  annotate("segment", x = 3.2, xend = 3.5, y = 0.7, yend = 0.5,
           linewidth = 1, color = "#424242") +
  annotate("segment", x = 3.8, xend = 3.5, y = 0.7, yend = 0.5,
           linewidth = 1, color = "#424242") +
  # Arrow (shows N-channel)
  annotate("segment", x = 2.1, xend = 2.4, y = 2, yend = 2,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm")), linewidth = 1, color = "#E41A1C") +
  # Terminal labels
  annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 2, label = "G (Gate)", size = 4, fontface = "bold", hjust = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = 4, y = 3.5, label = "D (Drain)", size = 4, fontface = "bold", hjust = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = 4, y = 0.5, label = "S (Source)", size = 4, fontface = "bold", hjust = 0) +
  # Device type
  annotate("text", x = 2.5, y = 4.2, label = "GaN HEMT (Depletion-Mode)", 
           size = 4.5, fontface = "bold", color = "#1976D2") +
  annotate("text", x = 2.5, y = 3.8, label = "3-Terminal Device Symbol", 
           size = 3.5, fontface = "italic") +
  labs(title = "Circuit Schematic Symbol") +
  xlim(0, 7) + ylim(0, 4.5) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 11))

# Arrange all four views
grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, ncol = 1, heights = c(1.2, 1, 1, 1))
```

**Key Properties:**

| Parameter | Typical Value | Significance |
|-----------|--------------|--------------|
| Sheet Density ($n_s$) | 1-2 × 10¹³ cm⁻² | High current capability |
| Electron Mobility ($\mu$) | 1500-2200 cm²/V·s | Fast switching, high gain |
| Sheet Resistance ($R_{sh}$) | 300-600 Ω/□ | Low on-resistance |
| 2DEG Confinement | <5 nm | Quantum effects dominant |

**Advantages:**

✅ **No Doping Required**: 2DEG forms naturally from polarization → fewer defects, higher mobility  
✅ **High Charge Density**: 10× higher than GaAs HEMTs → more current per unit width  
✅ **Confined Channel**: Reduces short-channel effects, enables scaling  
✅ **Temperature Stability**: Polarization-induced, less sensitive to temperature than doping

---

### Technology Comparison: GaN vs. LDMOS vs. GaAs

To appreciate GaN's advantages, it's essential to compare it with legacy RF power technologies.

```{r technology-cross-sections, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Cross-Sectional Comparison: GaN HEMT, LDMOS, and GaAs HEMT Structures [@pengelly2012; @mishra2002]", fig.width=12, fig.height=10}
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)

# Create three separate plots for each technology

# 1. GaN HEMT Cross-Section
create_gan_hemt_plot <- function() {
  ggplot() +
    # Substrate
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 10, ymin = 0, ymax = 1,
             fill = "#FFE0B2", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # Buffer layers
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 10, ymin = 1, ymax = 1.5,
             fill = "#FFF9C4", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    # GaN Channel
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 10, ymin = 1.5, ymax = 3.5,
             fill = "#C8E6C9", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # AlGaN Barrier
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 10, ymin = 3.5, ymax = 3.8,
             fill = "#90CAF9", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # Passivation
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 2, ymin = 3.8, ymax = 4.2,
             fill = "#E1BEE7", alpha = 0.6, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    annotate("rect", xmin = 4, xmax = 6, ymin = 3.8, ymax = 4.2,
             fill = "#E1BEE7", alpha = 0.6, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    annotate("rect", xmin = 8, xmax = 10, ymin = 3.8, ymax = 4.2,
             fill = "#E1BEE7", alpha = 0.6, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    # 2DEG (red line)
    annotate("segment", x = 0, xend = 10, y = 3.5, yend = 3.5,
             color = "red", linewidth = 2, linetype = "dashed") +
    # Source contact
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0.5, xmax = 1.5, ymin = 3.8, ymax = 4.5,
             fill = "#FFD54F", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # Gate contact
    annotate("rect", xmin = 4.5, xmax = 5.5, ymin = 3.8, ymax = 4.5,
             fill = "#A1887F", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # Drain contact
    annotate("rect", xmin = 8.5, xmax = 9.5, ymin = 3.8, ymax = 4.5,
             fill = "#FFD54F", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # Labels
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 0.5, label = "SiC or Si Substrate", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 1.25, label = "Buffer/Nucleation", size = 3) +
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 2.5, label = "GaN Channel (~1-2 μm)", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 3.65, label = "AlGaN Barrier (~25nm)", size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 7, y = 3.5, label = "2DEG", size = 3, color = "red", fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 1, y = 4.7, label = "Source", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 4.7, label = "Gate", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 9, y = 4.7, label = "Drain", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 5.2, label = "GaN HEMT (Heterojunction)", 
             size = 5, fontface = "bold", color = "#1976D2") +
    xlim(0, 10) + ylim(0, 5.5) +
    theme_void()
}

# 2. LDMOS Cross-Section
create_ldmos_plot <- function() {
  ggplot() +
    # Substrate (p-type)
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 10, ymin = 0, ymax = 1,
             fill = "#FFCCBC", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # N-well
    annotate("rect", xmin = 2, xmax = 8, ymin = 1, ymax = 2.5,
             fill = "#BBDEFB", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # P-body
    annotate("rect", xmin = 2.5, xmax = 5, ymin = 2.5, ymax = 3.5,
             fill = "#F8BBD0", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # N+ Source
    annotate("rect", xmin = 3, xmax = 4, ymin = 3.5, ymax = 4,
             fill = "#64B5F6", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # N+ Drain (in N-well)
    annotate("rect", xmin = 7, xmax = 8, ymin = 2.5, ymax = 3,
             fill = "#64B5F6", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # Gate oxide
    annotate("rect", xmin = 4.5, xmax = 6.5, ymin = 3.5, ymax = 3.6,
             fill = "#CFD8DC", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    # Gate poly
    annotate("rect", xmin = 4.3, xmax = 6.7, ymin = 3.6, ymax = 4.1,
             fill = "#A1887F", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # Field oxide (LOCOS)
    annotate("polygon", x = c(6.5, 7.5, 7.5, 6.5), y = c(3.6, 3.3, 2.5, 3.5),
             fill = "#CFD8DC", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.7) +
    # Source contact
    annotate("rect", xmin = 3.2, xmax = 3.8, ymin = 4, ymax = 4.5,
             fill = "#FFD54F", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # Drain contact
    annotate("rect", xmin = 7.2, xmax = 7.8, ymin = 3, ymax = 3.5,
             fill = "#FFD54F", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # Channel (dashed arrow)
    annotate("segment", x = 4, xend = 6.5, y = 3.45, yend = 3.2,
             color = "red", linewidth = 2, linetype = "dashed", 
             arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm"))) +
    # Labels
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 0.5, label = "p-Si Substrate", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 1.75, label = "N-Well (Drift Region)", size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 3.5, y = 3, label = "P-Body", size = 2.8) +
    annotate("text", x = 3.5, y = 3.75, label = "N+", size = 2.8, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 7.5, y = 2.75, label = "N+", size = 2.8, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 5.5, y = 3.85, label = "Gate", size = 3) +
    annotate("text", x = 5.5, y = 3.1, label = "Channel", size = 2.8, color = "red") +
    annotate("text", x = 3.5, y = 4.7, label = "Source", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 7.5, y = 3.7, label = "Drain", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 5.2, label = "LDMOS (Silicon MOSFET)", 
             size = 5, fontface = "bold", color = "#E64A19") +
    xlim(0, 10) + ylim(0, 5.5) +
    theme_void()
}

# 3. GaAs HEMT Cross-Section
create_gaas_hemt_plot <- function() {
  ggplot() +
    # GaAs Substrate
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 10, ymin = 0, ymax = 1,
             fill = "#FFECB3", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # GaAs Buffer
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 10, ymin = 1, ymax = 2,
             fill = "#FFE082", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    # GaAs Channel
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 10, ymin = 2, ymax = 3.5,
             fill = "#FDD835", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # AlGaAs Barrier (doped)
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 10, ymin = 3.5, ymax = 3.8,
             fill = "#F48FB1", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # GaAs Cap
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 2, ymin = 3.8, ymax = 4,
             fill = "#FFE082", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    annotate("rect", xmin = 4, xmax = 6, ymin = 3.8, ymax = 4,
             fill = "#FFE082", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    annotate("rect", xmin = 8, xmax = 10, ymin = 3.8, ymax = 4,
             fill = "#FFE082", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    # 2DEG (red line)
    annotate("segment", x = 0, xend = 10, y = 3.5, yend = 3.5,
             color = "red", linewidth = 2, linetype = "dashed") +
    # Source contact
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0.5, xmax = 1.5, ymin = 4, ymax = 4.5,
             fill = "#FFD54F", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # Gate contact (Schottky)
    annotate("rect", xmin = 4.5, xmax = 5.5, ymin = 3.8, ymax = 4.5,
             fill = "#A1887F", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # Drain contact
    annotate("rect", xmin = 8.5, xmax = 9.5, ymin = 4, ymax = 4.5,
             fill = "#FFD54F", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    # Labels
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 0.5, label = "Semi-insulating GaAs Substrate", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 1.5, label = "GaAs Buffer", size = 3) +
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 2.75, label = "GaAs Channel (undoped)", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 3.65, label = "AlGaAs Barrier (n-doped)", size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 7, y = 3.5, label = "2DEG", size = 3, color = "red", fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 1, y = 4.7, label = "Source", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 4.7, label = "Gate", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 9, y = 4.7, label = "Drain", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = 5, y = 5.2, label = "GaAs HEMT (Modulation Doping)", 
             size = 5, fontface = "bold", color = "#F57C00") +
    xlim(0, 10) + ylim(0, 5.5) +
    theme_void()
}

# Arrange plots vertically
p1 <- create_gan_hemt_plot()
p2 <- create_ldmos_plot()
p3 <- create_gaas_hemt_plot()

grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, ncol = 1)
```

**Key Structural Differences:**

1. **GaN HEMT**: 
   - Heterojunction (AlGaN/GaN) with polarization-induced 2DEG
   - No doping required in channel
   - Vertical structure with excellent heat dissipation

2. **LDMOS**: 
   - Lateral silicon MOSFET with extended drain
   - Doped channel (p-body with n+ source/drain)
   - Gate oxide controls surface inversion layer
   - Lower power density due to lower breakdown field

3. **GaAs HEMT**: 
   - Similar heterojunction concept but modulation-doped
   - Lower bandgap limits voltage capability
   - Semi-insulating substrate limits thermal performance

```{r technology-comparison-table, results='asis'}
# Comprehensive technology comparison table
tech_comparison <- data.frame(
  Parameter = c(
    "Bandgap (eV)",
    "Breakdown Field (MV/cm)",
    "Electron Saturation Velocity (×10⁷ cm/s)",
    "Thermal Conductivity (W/m·K)",
    "2DEG/Channel Sheet Density (×10¹³ cm⁻²)",
    "Electron Mobility (cm²/V·s)",
    "Sheet Resistance (Ω/□)",
    "Power Density (W/mm)",
    "Operating Voltage (V)",
    "Typical fT (GHz)",
    "Typical fmax (GHz)",
    "Relative Cost"
  ),
  GaN_HEMT = c(
    "3.4",
    "3.3",
    "2.5",
    "130-180 (SiC)",
    "1.0-2.0",
    "1500-2200",
    "300-600",
    "5-10",
    "28-50",
    "30-70",
    "100-200",
    "High"
  ),
  GaAs_HEMT = c(
    "1.43",
    "0.4",
    "2.0",
    "46",
    "0.8-1.5",
    "6000-9000",
    "200-400",
    "1-3",
    "8-12",
    "50-100",
    "150-300",
    "Medium-High"
  ),
  LDMOS = c(
    "1.12 (Si)",
    "0.3",
    "1.0",
    "150",
    "N/A (surface)",
    "400-600",
    "N/A",
    "1-2",
    "28-50",
    "5-15",
    "20-50",
    "Low"
  )
)

kable(tech_comparison,
      caption = "Performance Comparison: GaN HEMT vs. GaAs HEMT vs. LDMOS",
      col.names = c("Parameter", "GaN HEMT", "GaAs HEMT", "LDMOS"),
      align = "lccc") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 12) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = "#4CAF50", color = "white") %>%
  row_spec(c(1,2,3,4), background = "#E8F5E9") %>%  # Material properties
  row_spec(c(5,6,7), background = "#E3F2FD") %>%      # Electron transport
  row_spec(c(8,9), background = "#FFF9C4") %>%        # Power capability
  row_spec(c(10,11), background = "#F3E5F5") %>%      # RF performance
  column_spec(2, bold = TRUE, color = "#1976D2") %>%  # Highlight GaN
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "RF Power Transistor Technologies" = 3))
```

**Why GaN Wins for RF Power:**

| Advantage | GaN Benefit |
|-----------|-------------|
| **High Breakdown Field** | 10× higher than Si/GaAs → operate at higher voltages → higher RF power |
| **Wide Bandgap** | Lower leakage, high-temperature operation (up to 200°C junction) |
| **High 2DEG Density** | More current per mm → smaller devices for same power |
| **Excellent Thermal** | SiC substrate dissipates heat effectively → prevents thermal runaway |
| **High Saturation Velocity** | Fast switching → high gain at microwave frequencies |

This makes GaN the **technology of choice** for modern base station PAs operating at 2-6 GHz with 28-48V supplies.

---

## Operating Principles

### Charge Control and Modulation

The gate voltage modulates the 2DEG density by controlling the depletion region:

$$
I_D = q \mu n_s(V_{GS}) W \frac{dV_{ch}}{dx}
$$

**Variable Definitions**:

- $I_D$ [A] = Drain current (total current flowing from drain to source through channel)
- $q$ [C] = Elementary charge = 1.602 × 10⁻¹⁹ C
- $\mu$ [cm²/(V·s)] = Electron mobility in 2DEG (1500-2200 cm²/(V·s) for GaN, temperature dependent)
- $n_s(V_{GS})$ [cm⁻²] = 2DEG sheet density as function of gate voltage (modulated by gate)
- $W$ [mm] = Total gate width (device periphery, e.g., 12mm for large PA device)
- $\frac{dV_{ch}}{dx}$ [V/cm] = Channel voltage gradient along x-direction (drain-to-source axis)
- $V_{ch}$ [V] = Channel voltage at position x
- $x$ [cm] = Position along channel from source (x=0) to drain (x=L)

**Physical Interpretation**:
- This is the **drift-diffusion equation** for 2DEG current
- Current is proportional to: charge density ($q n_s$) × mobility ($\mu$) × electric field ($dV_{ch}/dx$) × width ($W$)
- Gate voltage $V_{GS}$ controls $n_s$ → modulates drain current → transconductance
- In saturation region: channel pinches off at drain end, but current continues via velocity saturation

**Operating Regions:**

1. **Linear/Triode Region** ($V_{DS} < V_{GS} - V_{th}$):
   - Channel acts as voltage-controlled resistor
   - $I_D \propto V_{DS}$ (ohmic behavior)
   - Used for switches and mixers

2. **Saturation Region** ($V_{DS} \geq V_{GS} - V_{th}$):
   - Channel pinches off at drain end
   - $I_D$ independent of $V_{DS}$
   - **RF amplifier operation region**
   - Transconductance: $g_m = \frac{\partial I_D}{\partial V_{GS}}$ is maximum

3. **Breakdown Region** ($V_{DS} > BV_{DSS}$):
   - Avalanche or impact ionization
   - Gate leakage increases dramatically
   - **MUST BE AVOIDED** in PA operation

### Transconductance and Current Control

**Transconductance** ($g_m$) determines gain and frequency response:

$$
g_m = \frac{\partial I_D}{\partial V_{GS}} \bigg|_{V_{DS}=const} \approx \mu \frac{W}{L} C_{ox} (V_{GS} - V_{th})
$$

**Variable Definitions**:

- $g_m$ [S or mS/mm] = Transconductance, rate of change of drain current with gate voltage ("trans" = across, conductance)
- $\frac{\partial I_D}{\partial V_{GS}}$ [A/V = S] = Partial derivative notation: change in $I_D$ for small change in $V_{GS}$ while holding $V_{DS}$ constant
- $V_{DS}$ [V] = Drain-source voltage (held constant during measurement)
- $\mu$ [cm²/(V·s)] = Electron mobility in channel
- $W$ [mm] = Gate width (device periphery)
- $L$ [µm] = Gate length (typically 0.25-1.0 µm for RF GaN HEMTs)
- $C_{ox}$ [F/cm²] = Gate oxide/barrier capacitance per unit area = $\varepsilon_r \varepsilon_0 / d$
- $V_{GS}$ [V] = Gate-source voltage (bias point)
- $V_{th}$ [V] = Threshold voltage (typically -6 to -3 V for depletion-mode GaN)

**Physical Interpretation**:
- $g_m$ represents **small-signal gain**: how effectively gate voltage controls drain current
- Higher $g_m$ → higher RF gain (voltage gain $A_v \propto g_m \times R_L$)
- Peak $g_m$ occurs near threshold voltage plus 1-2V
- Units: [S] = Siemens = 1/Ω = A/V, often expressed as mS/mm (normalized by gate width)
- Approximation assumes long-channel MOSFET model; GaN HEMTs may deviate due to velocity saturation

**Design Implications**:
- Class A bias: Set $V_{GS}$ at peak $g_m$ for maximum gain
- Class AB bias: Slightly below peak $g_m$ for efficiency-gain trade-off
- Larger W/L ratio → higher $g_m$ → higher gain, but also higher capacitance

**GaN Advantages:**
- High $g_m$ (200-500 mS/mm) due to high mobility and charge density
- Enables high gain at high frequencies
- Supports wideband operation

### High-Frequency Performance

**Key Figures of Merit:**

1. **Current-Gain Cutoff Frequency** ($f_T$):
   $$
   f_T = \frac{g_m}{2\pi (C_{gs} + C_{gd})} \approx \frac{v_{sat}}{2\pi L_g}
   $$
   
   **Variable Definitions**:
   
   - $f_T$ [Hz or GHz] = Current-gain cutoff frequency, frequency where short-circuit current gain $|h_{21}|$ = 1 (0 dB)
   - $g_m$ [S] = Transconductance (DC or low-frequency value)
   - $C_{gs}$ [pF] = Gate-source capacitance (intrinsic + overlap, typically 0.5-2 pF/mm)
   - $C_{gd}$ [pF] = Gate-drain capacitance (Miller capacitance, typically 0.05-0.2 pF/mm)
   - $(C_{gs} + C_{gd})$ [pF] = Total input capacitance that must be charged/discharged
   - $v_{sat}$ [cm/s] = Electron saturation velocity (≈ 2.5 × 10⁷ cm/s for GaN)
   - $L_g$ [µm] = Gate length (physical dimension of gate contact)
   - $2\pi$ [dimensionless] = Converts frequency to angular frequency
   
   **Physical Interpretation**:
   - $f_T$ is the frequency where transistor can no longer provide current gain
   - **First form** $\frac{g_m}{2\pi (C_{gs} + C_{gd})}$: time constant perspective
     - Larger $g_m$ → faster current response → higher $f_T$
     - Larger capacitance → slower charging → lower $f_T$
     - Gate capacitance acts as RC time constant limiting speed
   - **Second form** $\frac{v_{sat}}{2\pi L_g}$: transit time perspective
     - Electrons must transit gate length at saturation velocity
     - Transit time $\tau = L_g / v_{sat}$
     - $f_T = 1 / (2\pi \tau)$ = fundamental speed limit
   - Shorter gate length → faster transit → higher $f_T$ (but harder to fabricate)
   
   **Design Implications**:
   - Typical GaN: $f_T$ = 30-100 GHz (depends on gate length: 0.25 µm → 80 GHz, 1.0 µm → 25 GHz)
   - For RF PA at 3.5 GHz: need $f_T$ > 10× operating frequency → $f_T$ > 35 GHz
   - Determined by gate length and saturation velocity
   - GaN advantage: High $v_{sat}$ enables high $f_T$ even with longer gates (easier manufacturing)

2. **Maximum Oscillation Frequency** ($f_{max}$):
   $$
   f_{max} = \frac{f_T}{2\sqrt{R_g(R_{ds} + R_s) \cdot (2\pi f_T C_{gd})^2}}
   $$
   
   **Variable Definitions**:
   
   - $f_{max}$ [Hz or GHz] = Maximum oscillation frequency, frequency where **power gain** = 1 (0 dB)
   - $f_T$ [GHz] = Current-gain cutoff frequency (from previous equation)
   - $R_g$ [Ω] = Gate resistance (contact + spreading resistance, typically 1-5 Ω·mm)
   - $R_{ds}$ [Ω] = Drain-source output resistance (typically 200-800 Ω·mm in saturation)
   - $R_s$ [Ω] = Source resistance (contact + access resistance, typically 0.5-2 Ω·mm)
   - $(R_{ds} + R_s)$ [Ω] = Total output-side series resistance
   - $C_{gd}$ [pF] = Gate-drain feedback capacitance (Miller capacitance)
   - $(2\pi f_T C_{gd})$ [S] = Feedback admittance at $f_T$
   
   **Physical Interpretation**:
   - $f_{max}$ always **greater than** $f_T$ because power gain includes impedance transformation
   - Ratio $f_{max}/f_T$ typically 2-4× for GaN HEMTs (indicates quality of device)
   - **Resistive losses** degrade $f_{max}$:
     - $R_g$: Lossy input → reduces drive efficiency
     - $R_s$: Source degeneration → reduces effective $g_m$
     - $R_{ds}$: Output loss → reduces available power
   - **Feedback capacitance** $C_{gd}$: Creates unwanted coupling from output to input (Miller effect)
   - Square root dependence means $f_{max}$ degrades slower than $f_T$ with parasitics
   
   **Design Implications**:
   - Typical GaN: $f_{max}$ = 100-300 GHz (2-3× higher than $f_T$)
   - For power gain at 3.5 GHz: need $f_{max}$ > 10× operating frequency → $f_{max}$ > 35 GHz ✓
   - **Optimization strategies**:
     - Minimize $R_g$: Use T-gate or mushroom gate structures
     - Minimize $R_s$: Optimize source ohmic contacts (low contact resistance)
     - Minimize $C_{gd}$: Use field plates carefully (they increase $C_{gd}$)
     - Indicates power gain capability at high frequencies
   
```{r gan-small-signal-circuit, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Figure 1.2: GaN HEMT Small-Signal Equivalent Circuit [@gonzalez1997; @razavi2011]", fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# Create small-signal equivalent circuit diagram
library(ggplot2)

# Circuit element positions
circuit_elements <- data.frame(
  element = c("Rg", "Cgs", "Cgd", "gm_source", "Cds", "Rds", "Rs"),
  x = c(1, 2, 3, 3, 4, 4, 5),
  y = c(2, 2, 2.5, 2, 2, 1.5, 2),
  label = c("Rg", "Cgs", "Cgd", "gm·Vgs", "Cds", "Rds", "Rs"),
  value = c("1-5 Ω·mm", "0.5-2 pF/mm", "0.05-0.2 pF/mm", "200-500 mS/mm",
            "0.1-0.3 pF/mm", "200-800 Ω·mm", "0.5-2 Ω·mm")
)

# Create schematic representation
p <- ggplot() +
  # Gate terminal
  annotate("segment", x = 0, xend = 1, y = 2, yend = 2, linewidth = 1.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 2.3, label = "Gate", fontface = "bold", size = 4.5) +
  
  # Drain terminal  
  annotate("segment", x = 5, xend = 6, y = 2, yend = 2, linewidth = 1.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 6, y = 2.3, label = "Drain", fontface = "bold", size = 4.5) +
  
  # Source (ground)
  annotate("segment", x = 2.5, xend = 2.5, y = 1, yend = 0.5, linewidth = 1.5) +
  annotate("segment", x = 2.3, xend = 2.7, y = 0.5, yend = 0.5, linewidth = 2) +
  annotate("segment", x = 2.35, xend = 2.65, y = 0.4, yend = 0.4, linewidth = 1.5) +
  annotate("segment", x = 2.4, xend = 2.6, y = 0.3, yend = 0.3, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = 2.5, y = 0, label = "Source (GND)", fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  
  # Rg (resistor)
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0.8, xmax = 1.2, ymin = 1.9, ymax = 2.1, 
           fill = "#BBDEFB", color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = 1, y = 2.5, label = "Rg", size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 1, y = 1.5, label = circuit_elements$value[1], size = 3) +
  
  # Cgs (capacitor)
  annotate("segment", x = 1.95, xend = 1.95, y = 2, yend = 1.2, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("segment", x = 1.9, xend = 2.0, y = 1.6, yend = 1.6, linewidth = 2, color = "#1976D2") +
  annotate("segment", x = 1.9, xend = 2.0, y = 1.5, yend = 1.5, linewidth = 2, color = "#1976D2") +
  annotate("segment", x = 1.95, xend = 1.95, y = 1.5, yend = 1.2, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = 1.5, y = 1.55, label = "Cgs", size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 1.5, y = 1.2, label = circuit_elements$value[2], size = 3) +
  
  # Cgd (feedback capacitor)
  annotate("segment", x = 3, xend = 3, y = 2, yend = 2.3, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("segment", x = 2.95, xend = 3.05, y = 2.4, yend = 2.4, linewidth = 2, color = "#E41A1C") +
  annotate("segment", x = 2.95, xend = 3.05, y = 2.5, yend = 2.5, linewidth = 2, color = "#E41A1C") +
  annotate("segment", x = 3, xend = 4.5, y = 2.5, yend = 2.5, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("segment", x = 4.5, xend = 4.5, y = 2.5, yend = 2, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = 3.7, y = 2.8, label = "Cgd (Miller)", size = 4, 
           fontface = "bold", color = "#E41A1C") +
  annotate("text", x = 3.7, y = 3.1, label = circuit_elements$value[3], size = 3, color = "#E41A1C") +
  
  # Current source (gm)
  annotate("segment", x = 3.5, xend = 3.5, y = 2, yend = 1.2, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("segment", x = 3.5, xend = 3.5, y = 1.8, yend = 1.4, 
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm")), linewidth = 1.5, color = "#4CAF50") +
  annotate("text", x = 4.2, y = 1.6, label = "gm·Vgs", size = 4, 
           fontface = "bold", color = "#4CAF50") +
  annotate("text", x = 4.2, y = 1.2, label = circuit_elements$value[4], size = 3, color = "#4CAF50") +
  
  # Cds (output capacitance)
  annotate("segment", x = 4, xend = 4, y = 2, yend = 1.2, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("segment", x = 3.95, xend = 4.05, y = 1.7, yend = 1.7, linewidth = 2, color = "#1976D2") +
  annotate("segment", x = 3.95, xend = 4.05, y = 1.6, yend = 1.6, linewidth = 2, color = "#1976D2") +
  annotate("text", x = 4, y = 0.9, label = "Cds", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 4, y = 0.6, label = circuit_elements$value[5], size = 2.5) +
  
  # Rds (output resistance)
  annotate("rect", xmin = 4.4, xmax = 4.6, ymin = 1.55, ymax = 1.75, 
           fill = "#BBDEFB", color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("segment", x = 4.5, xend = 4.5, y = 2, yend = 1.75, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("segment", x = 4.5, xend = 4.5, y = 1.55, yend = 1.2, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = 4.5, y = 0.9, label = "Rds", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 4.5, y = 0.6, label = circuit_elements$value[6], size = 2.5) +
  
  # Horizontal connections
  annotate("segment", x = 1.2, xend = 1.95, y = 2, yend = 2, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("segment", x = 1.95, xend = 4.5, y = 2, yend = 2, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("segment", x = 4.5, xend = 5, y = 2, yend = 2, linewidth = 1) +
  
  # Ground connections
  annotate("segment", x = 1.95, xend = 2.5, y = 1.2, yend = 1, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("segment", x = 3.5, xend = 2.5, y = 1.2, yend = 1, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("segment", x = 4, xend = 2.5, y = 1.2, yend = 1, linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("segment", x = 4.5, xend = 2.5, y = 1.2, yend = 1, linewidth = 1) +
  
  # Annotations for fT and fmax
  annotate("text", x = 1, y = 3.5, 
           label = "Input: Rg limits speed\nCgs charges through Rg",
           size = 3.5, hjust = 0, color = "#1976D2") +
  annotate("text", x = 5.2, y = 3.5, 
           label = "Output: Rds + Rs limit fmax\nCds affects output impedance",
           size = 3.5, hjust = 1, color = "#D32F2F") +
  annotate("text", x = 3.7, y = 3.5, 
           label = "Feedback: Cgd (Miller)\ncouples output to input",
           size = 3.5, hjust = 0.5, color = "#E41A1C") +
  
  # Equations
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = 0.2, 
           label = "fT = gm / [2π(Cgs + Cgd)]     fmax = fT / [2√(Rg(Rds+Rs)·(2πfT·Cgd)²)]",
           size = 4, fontface = "italic", color = "#1976D2") +
  
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
  labs(title = "GaN HEMT Small-Signal Equivalent Circuit (Intrinsic Device)",
       subtitle = "Circuit model for analyzing fT and fmax - parasitics determine high-frequency performance") +
  xlim(-0.5, 6.5) +
  ylim(0, 4) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 13, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10))

print(p)
```
