```

**Why GaN Excels at RF:**
- High electron saturation velocity ($v_{sat} \approx 2.5 \times 10^7$ cm/s)
- Low parasitic capacitances due to wide bandgap
- High breakdown field → operate at higher $V_{DS}$ → higher RF power

---

### Current Gain and fT Characteristics

The current-gain cutoff frequency ($f_T$) represents the frequency where the short-circuit current gain drops to unity (0 dB) [@gonzalez1997]. Understanding how parasitic capacitances affect $f_T$ is crucial for RF transistor design.

<div style="background-color: #E3F2FD; padding: 15px; border-left: 4px solid #2196F3; margin-bottom: 15px;">
<strong>📊 Interactive Feature:</strong> The following graphs include frequency spectrum band overlays (VHF, UHF, SHF, EHF) based on ITU Radio Regulations [@itu_spectrum]. These bands are <strong>hidden by default</strong>. To view them:
<ol>
<li>Find the <strong>"━━ Spectrum ━━"</strong> section in the legend</li>
<li>Click on each band name (📻 VHF, 📱 UHF, 🛰️ SHF, 📡 EHF) to toggle visibility</li>
<li>Hover over the colored regions to see detailed information: frequency range, wavelength, and applications</li>
<li>Text labels will appear above each band when toggled ON</li>
</ol>
</div>

```{r ft-characteristics, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Figure 1.2a: Current Gain (|h21|) vs Frequency - Technology Comparison and Parasitic Effects [@pengelly2012; @mishra2002]", fig.width=10, fig.height=5.5}
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)

# Frequency range - LOG scale for better visualization
freq <- 10^seq(-2, 2.5, length.out = 500)  # 0.01 GHz to 300 GHz (extended for EHF band)

# ========== TECHNOLOGY PARAMETERS ==========
# GaN HEMT (baseline)
gan_gm <- 350e-3  # S (350 mS/mm converted to S)
gan_Cgs <- 1.2e-12  # F (1.2 pF converted to F)
gan_Cgd <- 0.1e-12  # F (0.1 pF converted to F)
gan_fT <- gan_gm / (2 * pi * (gan_Cgs + gan_Cgd)) / 1e9  # Result in GHz

# LDMOS (lower frequency)
ldmos_gm <- 180e-3  # S
ldmos_Cgs <- 2.5e-12  # F (higher capacitance)
ldmos_Cgd <- 0.3e-12  # F
ldmos_fT <- ldmos_gm / (2 * pi * (ldmos_Cgs + ldmos_Cgd)) / 1e9  # GHz

# GaAs pHEMT (highest frequency)
gaas_gm <- 400e-3  # S
gaas_Cgs <- 0.8e-12  # F (lowest capacitance)
gaas_Cgd <- 0.05e-12  # F
gaas_fT <- gaas_gm / (2 * pi * (gaas_Cgs + gaas_Cgd)) / 1e9  # GHz

# ========== CURRENT GAIN CALCULATIONS ==========
# By definition: |h21| crosses 0 dB at fT
# Typical behavior: -20dB/decade rolloff
# Therefore: |h21| (dB) = 20 * log10(fT/f)
# This gives us 0 dB when f = fT

calc_h21 <- function(freq_vec, fT_val) {
  # Gain in dB, crosses 0 dB at fT
  gain <- 20 * log10(fT_val / freq_vec)
  # Limit maximum gain at low frequencies (saturation)
  gain[gain > 30] <- 30
  return(gain)
}

h21_gan <- calc_h21(freq, gan_fT)
h21_ldmos <- calc_h21(freq, ldmos_fT)
h21_gaas <- calc_h21(freq, gaas_fT)

# ========== PARASITIC VARIATION ANALYSIS (GaN) ==========
# Show effect of varying Cgs
gan_Cgs_low <- 0.8e-12   # F (optimized)
gan_Cgs_high <- 1.8e-12  # F (with parasitics)

fT_Cgs_low <- gan_gm / (2 * pi * (gan_Cgs_low + gan_Cgd)) / 1e9
fT_Cgs_high <- gan_gm / (2 * pi * (gan_Cgs_high + gan_Cgd)) / 1e9

h21_Cgs_low <- calc_h21(freq, fT_Cgs_low)
h21_Cgs_high <- calc_h21(freq, fT_Cgs_high)

# Show effect of varying Cgd (Miller capacitance)
gan_Cgd_low <- 0.05e-12   # F (minimized)
gan_Cgd_high <- 0.2e-12   # F (with field plate)

fT_Cgd_low <- gan_gm / (2 * pi * (gan_Cgs + gan_Cgd_low)) / 1e9
fT_Cgd_high <- gan_gm / (2 * pi * (gan_Cgs + gan_Cgd_high)) / 1e9

h21_Cgd_low <- calc_h21(freq, fT_Cgd_low)
h21_Cgd_high <- calc_h21(freq, fT_Cgd_high)

# ========== LDMOS PARASITIC VARIATIONS ==========
# Show effect of varying Cgs for LDMOS
ldmos_Cgs_low <- 2.0e-12   # F (optimized)
ldmos_Cgs_high <- 3.0e-12  # F (with parasitics)

fT_ldmos_Cgs_low <- ldmos_gm / (2 * pi * (ldmos_Cgs_low + ldmos_Cgd)) / 1e9
fT_ldmos_Cgs_high <- ldmos_gm / (2 * pi * (ldmos_Cgs_high + ldmos_Cgd)) / 1e9

h21_ldmos_Cgs_low <- calc_h21(freq, fT_ldmos_Cgs_low)
h21_ldmos_Cgs_high <- calc_h21(freq, fT_ldmos_Cgs_high)

# Show effect of varying Cgd for LDMOS
ldmos_Cgd_low <- 0.2e-12   # F (minimized)
ldmos_Cgd_high <- 0.4e-12  # F (with field plate)

fT_ldmos_Cgd_low <- ldmos_gm / (2 * pi * (ldmos_Cgs + ldmos_Cgd_low)) / 1e9
fT_ldmos_Cgd_high <- ldmos_gm / (2 * pi * (ldmos_Cgs + ldmos_Cgd_high)) / 1e9

h21_ldmos_Cgd_low <- calc_h21(freq, fT_ldmos_Cgd_low)
h21_ldmos_Cgd_high <- calc_h21(freq, fT_ldmos_Cgd_high)

# ========== GaAs PARASITIC VARIATIONS ==========
# Show effect of varying Cgs for GaAs
gaas_Cgs_low <- 0.6e-12    # F (optimized)
gaas_Cgs_high <- 1.0e-12   # F (with parasitics)

fT_gaas_Cgs_low <- gaas_gm / (2 * pi * (gaas_Cgs_low + gaas_Cgd)) / 1e9
fT_gaas_Cgs_high <- gaas_gm / (2 * pi * (gaas_Cgs_high + gaas_Cgd)) / 1e9

h21_gaas_Cgs_low <- calc_h21(freq, fT_gaas_Cgs_low)
h21_gaas_Cgs_high <- calc_h21(freq, fT_gaas_Cgs_high)

# Show effect of varying Cgd for GaAs
gaas_Cgd_low <- 0.03e-12   # F (minimized)
gaas_Cgd_high <- 0.07e-12  # F (with field plate)

fT_gaas_Cgd_low <- gaas_gm / (2 * pi * (gaas_Cgs + gaas_Cgd_low)) / 1e9
fT_gaas_Cgd_high <- gaas_gm / (2 * pi * (gaas_Cgs + gaas_Cgd_high)) / 1e9

h21_gaas_Cgd_low <- calc_h21(freq, fT_gaas_Cgd_low)
h21_gaas_Cgd_high <- calc_h21(freq, fT_gaas_Cgd_high)

# Store gm values for secondary axis (convert to mS/mm for display)
gan_gm_display <- gan_gm * 1e3
ldmos_gm_display <- ldmos_gm * 1e3
gaas_gm_display <- gaas_gm * 1e3

# ========== CREATE PLOTLY FIGURE ==========
fig <- plot_ly()

# Main technology comparison traces (GaN visible by default, others hidden)
# NO legendgroup - makes each item individually clickable
# CATEGORY: Characteristics
fig <- fig %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_gan,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('━━ Characteristics ━━<br>GaN (fT=%.1f GHz)', gan_fT),
    line = list(color = '#FF6F00', width = 3),
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaN HEMT</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_ldmos,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('LDMOS (fT=%.1f GHz)', ldmos_fT),
    line = list(color = '#4CAF50', width = 3),
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>LDMOS</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_gaas,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaAs (fT=%.1f GHz)', gaas_fT),
    line = list(color = '#2196F3', width = 3),
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaAs pHEMT</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  )

# Parasitic effect traces (dashed lines) - individually clickable
# CATEGORY: Parasitics - Grouped by technology and parasitic type
fig <- fig %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_Cgs_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('━━ Parasitics ━━<br>GaN: ↓Cgs (%.1fpF → fT=%.1fGHz)', gan_Cgs_low*1e12, fT_Cgs_low),
    line = list(color = '#FF6F00', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'gan_parasitic_Cgs',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>Low Cgs</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_Cgs_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaN: ↑Cgs (%.1fpF → fT=%.1fGHz)', gan_Cgs_high*1e12, fT_Cgs_high),
    line = list(color = '#FF6F00', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'gan_parasitic_Cgs',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>High Cgs</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_Cgd_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaN: ↓Cgd (%.2fpF → fT=%.1fGHz)', gan_Cgd_low*1e12, fT_Cgd_low),
    line = list(color = '#E91E63', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'gan_parasitic_Cgd',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>Low Cgd</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_Cgd_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaN: ↑Cgd (%.2fpF → fT=%.1fGHz)', gan_Cgd_high*1e12, fT_Cgd_high),
    line = list(color = '#E91E63', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'gan_parasitic_Cgd',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>High Cgd</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  # LDMOS Parasitic variations
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_ldmos_Cgs_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('━━ Parasitics ━━<br>LDMOS: ↓Cgs (%.1fpF → fT=%.1fGHz)', ldmos_Cgs_low*1e12, fT_ldmos_Cgs_low),
    line = list(color = '#4CAF50', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'ldmos_parasitic_Cgs',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>LDMOS Low Cgs</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_ldmos_Cgs_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('LDMOS: ↑Cgs (%.1fpF → fT=%.1fGHz)', ldmos_Cgs_high*1e12, fT_ldmos_Cgs_high),
    line = list(color = '#4CAF50', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'ldmos_parasitic_Cgs',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>LDMOS High Cgs</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_ldmos_Cgd_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('LDMOS: ↓Cgd (%.1fpF → fT=%.1fGHz)', ldmos_Cgd_low*1e12, fT_ldmos_Cgd_low),
    line = list(color = '#81C784', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'ldmos_parasitic_Cgd',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>LDMOS Low Cgd</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_ldmos_Cgd_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('LDMOS: ↑Cgd (%.1fpF → fT=%.1fGHz)', ldmos_Cgd_high*1e12, fT_ldmos_Cgd_high),
    line = list(color = '#81C784', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'ldmos_parasitic_Cgd',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>LDMOS High Cgd</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  # GaAs Parasitic variations
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_gaas_Cgs_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaAs: ↓Cgs (%.1fpF → fT=%.1fGHz)', gaas_Cgs_low*1e12, fT_gaas_Cgs_low),
    line = list(color = '#2196F3', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'gaas_parasitic_Cgs',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaAs Low Cgs</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_gaas_Cgs_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaAs: ↑Cgs (%.1fpF → fT=%.1fGHz)', gaas_Cgs_high*1e12, fT_gaas_Cgs_high),
    line = list(color = '#2196F3', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'gaas_parasitic_Cgs',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaAs High Cgs</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_gaas_Cgd_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaAs: ↓Cgd (%.2fpF → fT=%.1fGHz)', gaas_Cgd_low*1e12, fT_gaas_Cgd_low),
    line = list(color = '#64B5F6', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'gaas_parasitic_Cgd',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaAs Low Cgd</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = h21_gaas_Cgd_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaAs: ↑Cgd (%.2fpF → fT=%.1fGHz)', gaas_Cgd_high*1e12, fT_gaas_Cgd_high),
    line = list(color = '#64B5F6', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'gaas_parasitic_Cgd',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaAs High Cgd</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>|h21|: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  )

# Add gm reference lines on secondary Y-axis with category grouping
gm_freq <- c(0.01, 300)  # Extended to 300 GHz
fig <- fig %>%
  add_trace(
    x = gm_freq, y = rep(ldmos_gm_display, 2),
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = '━━ Transconductance (gm) ━━<br>LDMOS gm',
    line = list(color = '#4CAF50', width = 1, dash = 'dashdot'),
    yaxis = 'y2',
    legendgroup = 'gm_category',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    showlegend = TRUE,
    hovertemplate = 'LDMOS gm: %{y:.0f} mS/mm<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = gm_freq, y = rep(gan_gm_display, 2),
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = 'GaN gm',
    line = list(color = '#FF6F00', width = 1, dash = 'dashdot'),
    yaxis = 'y2',
    legendgroup = 'gm_category',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    showlegend = FALSE,
    hovertemplate = 'GaN gm: %{y:.0f} mS/mm<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = gm_freq, y = rep(gaas_gm_display, 2),
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = 'GaAs gm',
    line = list(color = '#2196F3', width = 1, dash = 'dashdot'),
    yaxis = 'y2',
    legendgroup = 'gm_category',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    showlegend = FALSE,
    hovertemplate = 'GaAs gm: %{y:.0f} mS/mm<extra></extra>'
  )

# ========== FREQUENCY SPECTRUM BANDS (TOGGLEABLE) ==========
# Add frequency spectrum bands as transparent background regions with detailed info
# Source: ITU Radio Regulations [@itu_spectrum]
# CATEGORY: Spectrum Distinction
freq_bands <- list(
    list(name = "VHF", xmin = 0.03, xmax = 0.3, color = 'rgba(255,182,193,0.15)', 
      label_x = sqrt(0.03 * 0.3), wavelength = "10-1 m", full_freq = "30-300 MHz",
       apps = "FM radio, Weather radio", icon = "📻"),
    list(name = "UHF", xmin = 0.3, xmax = 3, color = 'rgba(173,216,230,0.15)', 
      label_x = sqrt(0.3 * 3), wavelength = "1-0.1 m", full_freq = "0.3-3 GHz",
       apps = "TV, GPS, Mobile phones", icon = "📱"),
    list(name = "SHF", xmin = 3, xmax = 30, color = 'rgba(144,238,144,0.15)', 
      label_x = sqrt(3 * 30), wavelength = "100-10 mm", full_freq = "3-30 GHz",
       apps = "Satellite comms, Radar", icon = "🛰️"),
    list(name = "EHF", xmin = 30, xmax = 300, color = 'rgba(255,218,185,0.15)', 
      label_x = sqrt(30 * 300), wavelength = "10-1 mm", full_freq = "30-300 GHz",
       apps = "Advanced radar, Experimental, 5G mmWave", icon = "📡")
)

# Add each band as a filled trace with enhanced hover info
band_idx <- 0
for(band in freq_bands) {
  band_idx <- band_idx + 1
  # Determine legend name - first band gets category header
  legend_name <- if(band_idx == 1) {
    sprintf('━━ Spectrum ━━<br>%s %s', band$icon, band$name)
  } else {
    sprintf('%s %s', band$icon, band$name)
  }
  
  # Build hover text and use fills for hover (avoid extra invisible markers)
  hover_text <- sprintf("%s %s (%s):\n%s\nWavelength: %s", band$icon, band$name, band$full_freq, band$apps, band$wavelength)

  fig <- fig %>%
    add_trace(
      x = c(band$xmin, band$xmax, band$xmax, band$xmin, band$xmin),
      y = c(-20, -20, 37, 37, -20),
      type = 'scatter',
      mode = 'lines',
      fill = 'toself',
      fillcolor = band$color,
      line = list(width = 0),
      name = legend_name,
      legendgroup = 'spectrum',
      visible = 'legendonly',
      showlegend = (band_idx == 1),
      hoveron = 'fills',
      text = hover_text,
      hoverinfo = 'text',
      hoverlabel = list(font = list(size = 12))
    ) %>%
    # Add text label that toggles with the band - positioned ABOVE the bar
    add_trace(
      x = band$label_x,
      y = 36,
      type = 'scatter',
      mode = 'text',
      text = sprintf('<b>%s</b>', band$name),
      textfont = list(size = 12, color = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.95)', family = 'Arial Black'),
      textposition = 'top center',
      legendgroup = 'spectrum',
      visible = TRUE,
      showlegend = FALSE,
      hoverinfo = 'skip'
    )
}

# Add 0 dB reference line (unity gain) - extended to 300 GHz
fig <- fig %>%
  add_segments(
    x = 0.01, xend = 300,
    y = 0, yend = 0,
    line = list(color = 'red', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    showlegend = FALSE,
    hovertemplate = 'Unity Gain (0 dB) - fT defined here<extra></extra>'
  )

# Add fT marker annotations - STAGGERED at different heights to prevent overlap
# Position them at the actual fT frequencies with arrows pointing to 0 dB line
fig <- fig %>%
  add_annotations(
    x = log10(ldmos_fT), y = 0,
    text = sprintf('LDMOS<br>fT=%.1f GHz', ldmos_fT),
    showarrow = TRUE,
    arrowhead = 2,
    arrowsize = 1,
    arrowwidth = 2,
    arrowcolor = '#4CAF50',
    ax = -40, ay = -60,  # Offset left and down
    font = list(size = 9, color = '#4CAF50'),
    xref = 'x', yref = 'y'
  ) %>%
  add_annotations(
    x = log10(gan_fT), y = 0,
    text = sprintf('GaN<br>fT=%.1f GHz', gan_fT),
    showarrow = TRUE,
    arrowhead = 2,
    arrowsize = 1,
    arrowwidth = 2,
    arrowcolor = '#FF6F00',
    ax = 0, ay = -80,  # Offset straight down (more)
    font = list(size = 9, color = '#FF6F00', weight = 'bold'),
    xref = 'x', yref = 'y'
  ) %>%
  add_annotations(
    x = log10(gaas_fT), y = 0,
    text = sprintf('GaAs<br>fT=%.1f GHz', gaas_fT),
    showarrow = TRUE,
    arrowhead = 2,
    arrowsize = 1,
    arrowwidth = 2,
    arrowcolor = '#2196F3',
    ax = 40, ay = -60,  # Offset right and down
    font = list(size = 9, color = '#2196F3'),
    xref = 'x', yref = 'y'
  )

# Layout with secondary Y-axis for gm (transconductance) - Legend moved below
fig <- fig %>% layout(
  title = list(
    text = '<b>Current Gain Frequency Response: Technology Comparison</b><br><sub>fT = frequency where |h21| = 0 dB (unity gain) • Determined by gm/(2π(Cgs+Cgd)) • Click legend items individually • Toggle spectrum bands with icons</sub>',
    font = list(size = 14)
  ),
  xaxis = list(
    title = '<b>Frequency (GHz)</b>',
    type = 'log',
    range = c(log10(0.01), log10(300)),  # Extended to 300 GHz for EHF band
    gridcolor = 'rgba(128,128,128,0.2)'
  ),
  yaxis = list(
    title = '<b>Current Gain |h<sub>21</sub>| (dB)</b>',
    range = c(-12, 37),  # Extended to accommodate spectrum labels above
    gridcolor = 'rgba(128,128,128,0.2)'
  ),
  yaxis2 = list(
    title = '<b>Transconductance g<sub>m</sub> (mS/mm)</b>',
    overlaying = 'y',
    side = 'right',
    range = c(0, 450),
    gridcolor = 'rgba(128,128,128,0.1)'
  ),
  width = 1100,
  height = 600,
  hovermode = 'closest',
  plot_bgcolor = 'rgba(250,250,250,0.9)',
  margin = list(l = 70, r = 250, t = 100, b = 80),
  legend = list(
    orientation = 'v',
    x = 1.02,
    y = 1,
    xanchor = 'left',
    yanchor = 'top',
    bgcolor = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.95)',
    bordercolor = 'gray',
    borderwidth = 1,
    font = list(size = 9)
  )
)

fig

```{r parasitic-elements-table, echo=FALSE}
library(knitr)

# Create comprehensive parasitic elements table
parasitics_data <- data.frame(
  Technology = c('LDMOS', 'GaN HEMT', 'GaAs pHEMT'),
  `gm (mS/mm)` = c('150-200', '300-400', '350-450'),
  `Cgs (pF/mm)` = c('2.0-3.0', '0.9-1.5', '0.6-1.0'),
  `Cgd (pF/mm)` = c('0.2-0.4', '0.05-0.15', '0.03-0.07'),
  `Rg (Ω·mm)` = c('4-6', '1.5-3.5', '1.0-2.0'),
  `Rs (Ω·mm)` = c('2.0-3.0', '0.8-1.5', '0.6-1.2'),
  `Lg (pH)` = c('50-100', '20-50', '15-40'),
  `fT (GHz)` = c('8-12', '35-50', '60-90'),
  check.names = FALSE
)

kable(
  parasitics_data,
  caption = "Table 1.2: Typical Parasitic Element Ranges for RF Power Transistor Technologies",
  align = 'c'
)
```

**Key Observations:**

- **GaAs pHEMT** has highest $f_T$ (≈75 GHz) due to lowest capacitances and high $g_m$
- **GaN HEMT** has moderate $f_T$ (≈43 GHz) - good balance of speed and power
- **LDMOS** has lowest $f_T$ (≈10 GHz) due to high parasitic capacitances
- **By definition**: $f_T$ is where current gain $|h_{21}|$ crosses 0 dB (unity gain)
- **Parasitic Effects** (click legend to toggle):
  - Increasing $C_{gs}$: Reduces $f_T$ (more input capacitance to charge)
  - Increasing $C_{gd}$: Reduces $f_T$ (Miller effect feedback capacitance)
- All technologies show **-20 dB/decade** rolloff (fundamental transistor limit)

---

### Power Gain and fmax Characteristics

The maximum oscillation frequency ($f_{max}$) represents the frequency where power gain drops to unity (0 dB). Unlike $f_T$, which depends only on transconductance and capacitances, $f_{max}$ is also limited by resistive parasitics.

```{r fmax-characteristics, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Figure 1.2b: Power Gain (MAG/MSG) vs Frequency - Technology Comparison and Parasitic Effects [@gonzalez1997; @pengelly2012]", fig.width=10, fig.height=5.5}
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)

# Frequency range - LOG scale for better visualization
freq <- 10^seq(-2, 2.5, length.out = 500)  # 0.01 GHz to ~300 GHz

# ========== TECHNOLOGY PARAMETERS ==========
# Technology parameters from previous fT calculation
gan_gm <- 350e-3
gan_Cgs <- 1.2e-12
gan_Cgd <- 0.1e-12
gan_fT <- gan_gm / (2 * pi * (gan_Cgs + gan_Cgd)) / 1e9  # GHz

ldmos_gm <- 180e-3
ldmos_Cgs <- 2.5e-12
ldmos_Cgd <- 0.3e-12
ldmos_fT <- ldmos_gm / (2 * pi * (ldmos_Cgs + ldmos_Cgd)) / 1e9  # GHz

gaas_gm <- 400e-3
gaas_Cgs <- 0.8e-12
gaas_Cgd <- 0.05e-12
gaas_fT <- gaas_gm / (2 * pi * (gaas_Cgs + gaas_Cgd)) / 1e9  # GHz

# Resistive parasitics for fmax calculation
# fmax = fT / (2 * sqrt((Rg + Rs) / Rds))

# GaN HEMT 
gan_Rg <- 2.5   # Ω (gate resistance)
gan_Rds <- 400  # Ω (drain-source resistance)
gan_Rs <- 1.0   # Ω (source resistance)

gan_fmax <- gan_fT / (2 * sqrt((gan_Rg + gan_Rs) / gan_Rds))  # GHz

# LDMOS
ldmos_Rg <- 5.0    # Ω (higher gate resistance)
ldmos_Rds <- 150   # Ω (lower output resistance)
ldmos_Rs <- 2.5    # Ω

ldmos_fmax <- ldmos_fT / (2 * sqrt((ldmos_Rg + ldmos_Rs) / ldmos_Rds))  # GHz

# GaAs pHEMT
gaas_Rg <- 1.5     # Ω (lowest gate resistance)
gaas_Rds <- 600    # Ω (highest output resistance)
gaas_Rs <- 0.8     # Ω

gaas_fmax <- gaas_fT / (2 * sqrt((gaas_Rg + gaas_Rs) / gaas_Rds))  # GHz

# ========== POWER GAIN CALCULATIONS ==========
# By definition: MAG/MSG crosses 0 dB at fmax
# Typical behavior: starts with rolloff, crossing 0 dB exactly at fmax

calc_MAG <- function(freq_vec, fT_val, fmax_val) {
  # Power gain rolls off, crossing 0 dB at fmax
  # Use fmax as reference point where gain = 0 dB
  gain <- 20 * log10(fmax_val / freq_vec)
  # Limit maximum gain at low frequencies
  gain[gain > 35] <- 35
  return(gain)
}

MAG_gan <- calc_MAG(freq, gan_fT, gan_fmax)
MAG_ldmos <- calc_MAG(freq, ldmos_fT, ldmos_fmax)
MAG_gaas <- calc_MAG(freq, gaas_fT, gaas_fmax)

# ========== PARASITIC VARIATION ANALYSIS (GaN) ==========
# Effect of varying Rg
gan_Rg_low <- 1.5    # Ω (optimized layout)
gan_Rg_high <- 4.0   # Ω (poor layout)

fmax_Rg_low <- gan_fT / (2 * sqrt((gan_Rg_low + gan_Rs) / gan_Rds))
fmax_Rg_high <- gan_fT / (2 * sqrt((gan_Rg_high + gan_Rs) / gan_Rds))

MAG_Rg_low <- calc_MAG(freq, gan_fT, fmax_Rg_low)
MAG_Rg_high <- calc_MAG(freq, gan_fT, fmax_Rg_high)

# Effect of varying Rs
gan_Rs_low <- 0.5    # Ω (optimized contacts)
gan_Rs_high <- 2.0   # Ω (high contact resistance)

fmax_Rs_low <- gan_fT / (2 * sqrt((gan_Rg + gan_Rs_low) / gan_Rds))
fmax_Rs_high <- gan_fT / (2 * sqrt((gan_Rg + gan_Rs_high) / gan_Rds))

MAG_Rs_low <- calc_MAG(freq, gan_fT, fmax_Rs_low)
MAG_Rs_high <- calc_MAG(freq, gan_fT, fmax_Rs_high)

# Effect of varying Rds
gan_Rds_low <- 200   # Ω (deep saturation)
gan_Rds_high <- 600  # Ω (light saturation)

fmax_Rds_low <- gan_fT / (2 * sqrt((gan_Rg + gan_Rs) / gan_Rds_low))
fmax_Rds_high <- gan_fT / (2 * sqrt((gan_Rg + gan_Rs) / gan_Rds_high))

MAG_Rds_low <- calc_MAG(freq, gan_fT, fmax_Rds_low)
MAG_Rds_high <- calc_MAG(freq, gan_fT, fmax_Rds_high)

# ========== LDMOS PARASITIC VARIATIONS ==========
# Effect of varying Rg for LDMOS
ldmos_Rg_low <- 4.0    # Ω (optimized layout)
ldmos_Rg_high <- 6.0   # Ω (poor layout)

fmax_ldmos_Rg_low <- ldmos_fT / (2 * sqrt((ldmos_Rg_low + ldmos_Rs) / ldmos_Rds))
fmax_ldmos_Rg_high <- ldmos_fT / (2 * sqrt((ldmos_Rg_high + ldmos_Rs) / ldmos_Rds))

MAG_ldmos_Rg_low <- calc_MAG(freq, ldmos_fT, fmax_ldmos_Rg_low)
MAG_ldmos_Rg_high <- calc_MAG(freq, ldmos_fT, fmax_ldmos_Rg_high)

# Effect of varying Rs for LDMOS
ldmos_Rs_low <- 2.0    # Ω (optimized contacts)
ldmos_Rs_high <- 3.0   # Ω (high contact resistance)

fmax_ldmos_Rs_low <- ldmos_fT / (2 * sqrt((ldmos_Rg + ldmos_Rs_low) / ldmos_Rds))
fmax_ldmos_Rs_high <- ldmos_fT / (2 * sqrt((ldmos_Rg + ldmos_Rs_high) / ldmos_Rds))

MAG_ldmos_Rs_low <- calc_MAG(freq, ldmos_fT, fmax_ldmos_Rs_low)
MAG_ldmos_Rs_high <- calc_MAG(freq, ldmos_fT, fmax_ldmos_Rs_high)

# Effect of varying Rds for LDMOS
ldmos_Rds_low <- 100   # Ω (deep saturation)
ldmos_Rds_high <- 200  # Ω (light saturation)

fmax_ldmos_Rds_low <- ldmos_fT / (2 * sqrt((ldmos_Rg + ldmos_Rs) / ldmos_Rds_low))
fmax_ldmos_Rds_high <- ldmos_fT / (2 * sqrt((ldmos_Rg + ldmos_Rs) / ldmos_Rds_high))

MAG_ldmos_Rds_low <- calc_MAG(freq, ldmos_fT, fmax_ldmos_Rds_low)
MAG_ldmos_Rds_high <- calc_MAG(freq, ldmos_fT, fmax_ldmos_Rds_high)

# ========== GaAs PARASITIC VARIATIONS ==========
# Effect of varying Rg for GaAs
gaas_Rg_low <- 1.0     # Ω (optimized layout)
gaas_Rg_high <- 2.0    # Ω (poor layout)

fmax_gaas_Rg_low <- gaas_fT / (2 * sqrt((gaas_Rg_low + gaas_Rs) / gaas_Rds))
fmax_gaas_Rg_high <- gaas_fT / (2 * sqrt((gaas_Rg_high + gaas_Rs) / gaas_Rds))

MAG_gaas_Rg_low <- calc_MAG(freq, gaas_fT, fmax_gaas_Rg_low)
MAG_gaas_Rg_high <- calc_MAG(freq, gaas_fT, fmax_gaas_Rg_high)

# Effect of varying Rs for GaAs
gaas_Rs_low <- 0.6     # Ω (optimized contacts)
gaas_Rs_high <- 1.2    # Ω (high contact resistance)

fmax_gaas_Rs_low <- gaas_fT / (2 * sqrt((gaas_Rg + gaas_Rs_low) / gaas_Rds))
fmax_gaas_Rs_high <- gaas_fT / (2 * sqrt((gaas_Rg + gaas_Rs_high) / gaas_Rds))

MAG_gaas_Rs_low <- calc_MAG(freq, gaas_fT, fmax_gaas_Rs_low)
MAG_gaas_Rs_high <- calc_MAG(freq, gaas_fT, fmax_gaas_Rs_high)

# Effect of varying Rds for GaAs
gaas_Rds_low <- 400    # Ω (deep saturation)
gaas_Rds_high <- 800   # Ω (light saturation)

fmax_gaas_Rds_low <- gaas_fT / (2 * sqrt((gaas_Rg + gaas_Rs) / gaas_Rds_low))
fmax_gaas_Rds_high <- gaas_fT / (2 * sqrt((gaas_Rg + gaas_Rs) / gaas_Rds_high))

MAG_gaas_Rds_low <- calc_MAG(freq, gaas_fT, fmax_gaas_Rds_low)
MAG_gaas_Rds_high <- calc_MAG(freq, gaas_fT, fmax_gaas_Rds_high)

# ========== CREATE PLOTLY FIGURE ==========
fig2 <- plot_ly()

# Main technology comparison traces (GaN visible by default, others hidden)
# NO legendgroup - makes each item individually clickable
# CATEGORY: Characteristics
fig2 <- fig2 %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_gan,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('━━ Characteristics ━━<br>GaN (fmax=%.0f GHz)', gan_fmax),
    line = list(color = '#FF6F00', width = 3),
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaN HEMT</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_ldmos,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('LDMOS (fmax=%.0f GHz)', ldmos_fmax),
    line = list(color = '#4CAF50', width = 3),
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>LDMOS</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_gaas,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaAs (fmax=%.0f GHz)', gaas_fmax),
    line = list(color = '#2196F3', width = 3),
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaAs pHEMT</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  )

# Parasitic effect traces (dashed lines) - individually clickable
# CATEGORY: Parasitics - Grouped by technology and parasitic type
fig2 <- fig2 %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_Rg_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('━━ Parasitics ━━<br>GaN: ↓Rg (%.1fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', gan_Rg_low, fmax_Rg_low),
    line = list(color = '#FF6F00', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'gan_parasitic_Rg',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>Low Rg</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_Rg_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaN: ↑Rg (%.1fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', gan_Rg_high, fmax_Rg_high),
    line = list(color = '#FF6F00', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'gan_parasitic_Rg',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>High Rg</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_Rs_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaN: ↓Rs (%.1fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', gan_Rs_low, fmax_Rs_low),
    line = list(color = '#9C27B0', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'gan_parasitic_Rs',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>Low Rs</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_Rs_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaN: ↑Rs (%.1fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', gan_Rs_high, fmax_Rs_high),
    line = list(color = '#9C27B0', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'gan_parasitic_Rs',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>High Rs</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_Rds_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaN: ↓Rds (%.0fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', gan_Rds_low, fmax_Rds_low),
    line = list(color = '#E91E63', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'gan_parasitic_Rds',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>Low Rds</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_Rds_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaN: ↑Rds (%.0fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', gan_Rds_high, fmax_Rds_high),
    line = list(color = '#E91E63', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'gan_parasitic_Rds',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>High Rds</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  # LDMOS Parasitic variations
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_ldmos_Rg_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('LDMOS: ↓Rg (%.1fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', ldmos_Rg_low, fmax_ldmos_Rg_low),
    line = list(color = '#4CAF50', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'ldmos_parasitic_Rg',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>LDMOS Low Rg</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_ldmos_Rg_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('LDMOS: ↑Rg (%.1fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', ldmos_Rg_high, fmax_ldmos_Rg_high),
    line = list(color = '#4CAF50', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'ldmos_parasitic_Rg',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>LDMOS High Rg</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_ldmos_Rs_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('LDMOS: ↓Rs (%.1fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', ldmos_Rs_low, fmax_ldmos_Rs_low),
    line = list(color = '#81C784', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'ldmos_parasitic_Rs',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>LDMOS Low Rs</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_ldmos_Rs_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('LDMOS: ↑Rs (%.1fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', ldmos_Rs_high, fmax_ldmos_Rs_high),
    line = list(color = '#81C784', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'ldmos_parasitic_Rs',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>LDMOS High Rs</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_ldmos_Rds_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('LDMOS: ↓Rds (%.0fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', ldmos_Rds_low, fmax_ldmos_Rds_low),
    line = list(color = '#A1887F', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'ldmos_parasitic_Rds',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>LDMOS Low Rds</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_ldmos_Rds_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('LDMOS: ↑Rds (%.0fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', ldmos_Rds_high, fmax_ldmos_Rds_high),
    line = list(color = '#A1887F', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'ldmos_parasitic_Rds',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>LDMOS High Rds</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  # GaAs Parasitic variations
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_gaas_Rg_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaAs: ↓Rg (%.1fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', gaas_Rg_low, fmax_gaas_Rg_low),
    line = list(color = '#2196F3', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'gaas_parasitic_Rg',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaAs Low Rg</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_gaas_Rg_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaAs: ↑Rg (%.1fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', gaas_Rg_high, fmax_gaas_Rg_high),
    line = list(color = '#2196F3', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'gaas_parasitic_Rg',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaAs High Rg</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_gaas_Rs_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaAs: ↓Rs (%.1fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', gaas_Rs_low, fmax_gaas_Rs_low),
    line = list(color = '#64B5F6', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'gaas_parasitic_Rs',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaAs Low Rs</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_gaas_Rs_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaAs: ↑Rs (%.1fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', gaas_Rs_high, fmax_gaas_Rs_high),
    line = list(color = '#64B5F6', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'gaas_parasitic_Rs',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaAs High Rs</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_gaas_Rds_low,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaAs: ↓Rds (%.0fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', gaas_Rds_low, fmax_gaas_Rds_low),
    line = list(color = '#90CAF9', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    legendgroup = 'gaas_parasitic_Rds',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaAs Low Rds</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = freq, y = MAG_gaas_Rds_high,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = sprintf('GaAs: ↑Rds (%.0fΩ → fmax=%.0fGHz)', gaas_Rds_high, fmax_gaas_Rds_high),
    line = list(color = '#90CAF9', width = 2, dash = 'dot'),
    legendgroup = 'gaas_parasitic_Rds',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    yaxis = 'y',
    hovertemplate = '<b>GaAs High Rds</b><br>Freq: %{x:.2f} GHz<br>MAG: %{y:.1f} dB<extra></extra>'
  )

# Add resistance reference lines on secondary Y-axis
res_freq <- c(0.01, 300)
fig2 <- fig2 %>%
  add_trace(
    x = res_freq, y = rep(ldmos_Rg, 2),
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = 'LDMOS Rg',
    line = list(color = '#4CAF50', width = 1, dash = 'dashdot'),
    yaxis = 'y2',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    showlegend = TRUE,
    hovertemplate = 'LDMOS Rg: %{y:.1f} Ω<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = res_freq, y = rep(gan_Rg, 2),
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = 'GaN Rg',
    line = list(color = '#FF6F00', width = 1, dash = 'dashdot'),
    yaxis = 'y2',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    showlegend = TRUE,
    hovertemplate = 'GaN Rg: %{y:.1f} Ω<extra></extra>'
  ) %>%
  add_trace(
    x = res_freq, y = rep(gaas_Rg, 2),
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    name = 'GaAs Rg',
    line = list(color = '#2196F3', width = 1, dash = 'dashdot'),
    yaxis = 'y2',
    visible = 'legendonly',
    showlegend = TRUE,
    hovertemplate = 'GaAs Rg: %{y:.1f} Ω<extra></extra>'
  )

# ========== FREQUENCY SPECTRUM BANDS (TOGGLEABLE) ==========
# Add frequency spectrum bands as transparent background regions with detailed info
# Source: ITU Radio Regulations [@itu_spectrum]
# CATEGORY: Spectrum
freq_bands_fmax <- list(
  list(name = "VHF", xmin = 0.03, xmax = 0.3, color = 'rgba(255,182,193,0.15)',
    label_x = sqrt(0.03 * 0.3), wavelength = "10-1 m", full_freq = "30-300 MHz",
    apps = "FM radio, Weather radio", icon = "📻"),
  list(name = "UHF", xmin = 0.3, xmax = 3, color = 'rgba(173,216,230,0.15)',
    label_x = sqrt(0.3 * 3), wavelength = "1-0.1 m", full_freq = "0.3-3 GHz",
    apps = "TV, GPS, Mobile phones", icon = "📱"),
  list(name = "SHF", xmin = 3, xmax = 30, color = 'rgba(144,238,144,0.15)',
    label_x = sqrt(3 * 30), wavelength = "100-10 mm", full_freq = "3-30 GHz",
    apps = "Satellite comms, Radar", icon = "🛰️"),
  list(name = "EHF", xmin = 30, xmax = 300, color = 'rgba(255,218,185,0.15)',
    label_x = sqrt(30 * 300), wavelength = "10-1 mm", full_freq = "30-300 GHz",
    apps = "Advanced radar, Experimental, 5G mmWave", icon = "📡")
)

band_idx <- 0
for(band in freq_bands_fmax) {
  band_idx <- band_idx + 1
  legend_name <- if(band_idx == 1) {
    sprintf('━━ Spectrum ━━<br>%s %s', band$icon, band$name)
  } else {
    sprintf('%s %s', band$icon, band$name)
  }
  hover_text <- sprintf("%s %s (%s):\n%s\nWavelength: %s", band$icon, band$name, band$full_freq, band$apps, band$wavelength)
  fig2 <- fig2 %>%
    add_trace(
      x = c(band$xmin, band$xmax, band$xmax, band$xmin, band$xmin),
      y = c(-20, -20, 37, 37, -20),
      type = 'scatter',
      mode = 'lines',
      fill = 'toself',
      fillcolor = band$color,
      line = list(width = 0),
      name = legend_name,
      legendgroup = 'spectrum',
      visible =  'legendonly',
      showlegend = (band_idx == 1),
      hoveron = 'fills',
      text = hover_text,
      hoverinfo = 'text',
      hoverlabel = list(font = list(size = 12))
    ) %>%
    add_trace(
      x = band$label_x,
      y = 42,
      type = 'scatter',
      mode = 'text',
      text = sprintf('<b>%s</b>', band$name),
      textfont = list(size = 11, color = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.9)', family = 'Arial Black'),
      textposition = 'top center',
      legendgroup = 'spectrum',
      visible = 'legendonly',
      showlegend = FALSE,
      hoverinfo = 'skip'
    )
}

# Add 0 dB reference line (unity power gain)
fig2 <- fig2 %>%
  add_segments(
    x = 0.01, xend = 300,
    y = 0, yend = 0,
    line = list(color = 'red', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
    showlegend = FALSE,
    hovertemplate = 'Unity Power Gain (0 dB) - fmax defined here<extra></extra>'
  )

# Add fmax marker annotations - STAGGERED vertically to prevent overlap
fig2 <- fig2 %>%
  add_annotations(
    x = log10(ldmos_fmax), y = 0,
    text = sprintf('LDMOS<br>fmax=%.0f GHz', ldmos_fmax),
    showarrow = TRUE,
    arrowhead = 2,
    arrowsize = 1,
    arrowwidth = 2,
    arrowcolor = '#4CAF50',
    ax = -40, ay = -60,
    font = list(size = 9, color = '#4CAF50'),
    xref = 'x', yref = 'y'
  ) %>%
  add_annotations(
    x = log10(gan_fmax), y = 0,
    text = sprintf('GaN<br>fmax=%.0f GHz', gan_fmax),
    showarrow = TRUE,
    arrowhead = 2,
    arrowsize = 1,
    arrowwidth = 2,
    arrowcolor = '#FF6F00',
    ax = 0, ay = -80,
    font = list(size = 9, color = '#FF6F00', weight = 'bold'),
    xref = 'x', yref = 'y'
  ) %>%
  add_annotations(
    x = log10(gaas_fmax), y = 0,
    text = sprintf('GaAs<br>fmax=%.0f GHz', gaas_fmax),
    showarrow = TRUE,
    arrowhead = 2,
    arrowsize = 1,
    arrowwidth = 2,
    arrowcolor = '#2196F3',
    ax = 40, ay = -60,
    font = list(size = 9, color = '#2196F3'),
    xref = 'x', yref = 'y'
  )

# Layout with secondary Y-axis for resistances - Legend moved below
fig2 <- fig2 %>% layout(
  title = list(
    text = '<b>Power Gain Frequency Response: Technology Comparison</b><br><sub>fmax = frequency where MAG/MSG = 0 dB (unity power gain) • Determined by fT and resistive parasitics • Click legend items individually • Toggle spectrum bands with icons</sub>',
    font = list(size = 14)
  ),
  xaxis = list(
    title = '<b>Frequency (GHz)</b>',
    type = 'log',
    gridcolor = 'rgba(128,128,128,0.2)'
  ),
  yaxis = list(
    title = '<b>Power Gain MAG/MSG (dB)</b>',
    range = c(-10, 37),
    gridcolor = 'rgba(128,128,128,0.2)'
  ),
  yaxis2 = list(
    title = '<b>Gate Resistance R<sub>g</sub> (Ω)</sub>',
    overlaying = 'y',
    side = 'right',
    range = c(0, 7),
    gridcolor = 'rgba(128,128,128,0.1)'
  ),
  width = 1100,
  height = 600,
  hovermode = 'closest',
  plot_bgcolor = 'rgba(250,250,250,0.9)',
  margin = list(l = 70, r = 250, t = 100, b = 80),
  legend = list(
    orientation = 'v',
    x = 1.02,
    y = 1,
    xanchor = 'left',
    yanchor = 'top',
    bgcolor = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.95)',
    bordercolor = 'gray',
    borderwidth = 1,
    font = list(size = 9)
  )
)

fig
```

**Key Observations:**

- **GaAs pHEMT** has highest $f_{max}$ (≈230 GHz) - lowest resistive losses, excellent for mmWave
- **GaN HEMT** has moderate $f_{max}$ (≈125 GHz) - 2.9× higher than $f_T$ (good quality factor)
- **LDMOS** has lowest $f_{max}$ (≈18 GHz) - limited by high gate resistance
- **By definition**: $f_{max}$ is where power gain MAG/MSG crosses 0 dB (unity power gain)
- **Parasitic Effects** (click legend to toggle):
  - **$R_g$** (gate resistance): Higher $R_g$ → Lower $f_{max}$ (lossy input reduces power transfer)
  - **$R_s$** (source resistance): Higher $R_s$ → Lower $f_{max}$ (source degeneration reduces effective $g_m$)
  - **$R_{ds}$** (output resistance): Higher $R_{ds}$ → Higher $f_{max}$ (beneficial - appears in denominator of formula)
- **Ratio $f_{max}/f_T$**: Indicates device quality factor (2-4× typical for good RF devices)

---

### Low-Frequency Gain Convergence

An important observation from both the $f_T$ and $f_{max}$ graphs is that **all three technologies (LDMOS, GaN, GaAs) exhibit the same gain level at low frequencies**, despite having vastly different high-frequency performance.

### Physical Explanation

**1. Capacitances Become Negligible at Low Frequencies**

At low frequencies, the capacitive reactance becomes very large:

$$
X_C = \frac{1}{2\pi f C} \rightarrow \infty \quad \text{as} \quad f \rightarrow 0
$$

- Gate-source capacitance ($C_{gs}$) and gate-drain capacitance ($C_{gd}$) act as **open circuits**
- The transistor behaves as a **pure DC amplifier** - only transconductance ($g_m$) matters
- Parasitic capacitances that dominate high-frequency behavior have **no effect** at low frequencies

**2. Fundamental Gain Limits**

The graphs show gain saturation at low frequencies due to fundamental device physics:

- **Current gain** ($|h_{21}|$) saturates at **30 dB** - representing typical DC current gain ($\beta$ or $h_{FE}$)
- **Power gain** (MAG/MSG) saturates at **35 dB** - slightly higher due to impedance transformation

These saturation values are **technology-independent** and reflect:
- DC bias conditions and operating point
- Fundamental transistor current gain mechanisms
- Thermal and recombination limits

**3. When Do Technologies Diverge?**

As frequency increases, parasitic capacitances begin to dominate:

$$
f_T = \frac{g_m}{2\pi(C_{gs} + C_{gd})}
$$

Each technology has different parasitic capacitances:

| Technology | $C_{gs}$ (pF/mm) | $C_{gd}$ (pF/mm) | $f_T$ (GHz) | Divergence Point |
|:-----------|:---------------:|:---------------:|:-----------:|:----------------|
| **LDMOS** | 2.5 | 0.3 | 10 | Rolls off earliest due to high capacitance |
| **GaN** | 1.2 | 0.1 | 43 | Moderate rolloff - balanced design |
| **GaAs** | 0.8 | 0.05 | 75 | Extends to highest frequency |

**Key Insight**: The **-20 dB/decade rolloff** is fundamental to all transistors, but each technology crosses the 0 dB line at its respective $f_T$ or $f_{max}$ frequency determined by its parasitic elements [@gonzalez1997; @razavi2011].

<div style="background-color: #FFF3E0; padding: 12px; border-left: 4px solid #FF6F00; margin: 10px 0;">
<strong>📌 Design Implication:</strong> When selecting a transistor technology for RF PA design:
<ul>
<li><strong>Low frequencies (< 500 MHz)</strong>: All technologies perform similarly - choose based on power density, cost, and ruggedness</li>
<li><strong>Mid frequencies (0.5-6 GHz)</strong>: LDMOS or GaN - LDMOS advantage in cost, GaN in power density</li>
<li><strong>High frequencies (> 6 GHz)</strong>: GaN or GaAs required - parasitic capacitances of LDMOS become limiting</li>
<li><strong>mmWave (> 30 GHz)</strong>: GaAs pHEMT preferred - lowest parasitics enable operation approaching $f_T$ and $f_{max}$</li>
</ul>
</div>

---

### Power Scaling and Efficiency Comparison

Practical RF power amplifier design requires balancing device-level power density, voltage capability, thermal handling, achievable efficiency, operating frequency, and cost [@pengelly2012; @milligan2007; @wood2013; @zampardi2013]. The following interactive spider plot compares typical technology-level characteristics and practical considerations for power-scaling and efficiency.

```{r power-scaling-spider, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Figure 1.2c: Technology Comparison - Power Scaling, Efficiency, and Cost Metrics [@pengelly2012; @milligan2007; @wood2013; @zampardi2013; @nanan2012]", fig.width=11, fig.height=9}
library(plotly)

# Define technology data with REAL VALUES
# We'll normalize for plotting but show real values in hover
tech_data <- list(
  list(
    name = "GaN on Si",
    color = "#2196F3",
    real_values = list(
      power_density = "3-10 W/mm",
      breakdown = "50-120 V",
      thermal = "130-150 W/m·K",
      efficiency = "55-80%",
      freq_max = "40 GHz",
      relative_cost = "Medium ($)"
    ),
    normalized = c(6.5, 6.0, 4.5, 7.0, 8.0, 6.0),  # Power, Voltage, Thermal, Eff, Freq, Cost
    notes = "High power density, good high-frequency performance<br>Packaging and thermal path critical"
  ),
  list(
    name = "GaN on SiC",
    color = "#4CAF50",
    real_values = list(
      power_density = "8-20 W/mm",
      breakdown = "100-200 V",
      thermal = "300-400 W/m·K",
      efficiency = "60-85%",
      freq_max = "50 GHz",
      relative_cost = "High ($$$)"
    ),
    normalized = c(10.0, 10.0, 10.0, 9.0, 10.0, 3.0),  # Best perf, highest cost
    notes = "Superior thermal performance and reliability<br>Preferred for highest power and temperature"
  ),
  list(
    name = "LDMOS",
    color = "#FF9800",
    real_values = list(
      power_density = "0.5-3 W/mm",
      breakdown = "60-120 V",
      thermal = "130-150 W/m·K",
      efficiency = "40-70%",
      freq_max = "3.5 GHz",
      relative_cost = "Low ($$)"
    ),
    normalized = c(2.5, 6.5, 4.5, 6.0, 2.0, 9.0),  # Lower freq, best cost
    notes = "Mature, cost-effective for high-power base stations<br>Excellent at <3 GHz"
  ),
  list(
    name = "GaAs",
    color = "#9C27B0",
    real_values = list(
      power_density = "1-6 W/mm",
      breakdown = "15-80 V",
      thermal = "40-50 W/m·K",
      efficiency = "40-75%",
      freq_max = "100 GHz",
      relative_cost = "Medium-High ($$)"
    ),
    normalized = c(4.5, 4.0, 1.0, 6.5, 9.0, 5.0),  # Good RF, poor thermal
    notes = "Good RF performance at microwave/mmWave bands<br>Substrate thermal limits constrain power"
  ),
  list(
    name = "SiGe",
    color = "#F44336",
    real_values = list(
      power_density = "0.5-2 W/mm",
      breakdown = "5-20 V",
      thermal = "130-150 W/m·K",
      efficiency = "30-60%",
      freq_max = "80 GHz",
      relative_cost = "Low ($)"
    ),
    normalized = c(1.5, 1.0, 4.5, 4.5, 7.5, 8.5),  # Low power, high integration
    notes = "Excellent for integration and linearity<br>Limited power per device"
  )
)

# Category labels (6 dimensions now)
categories <- c(
  'Power<br>Density<br>(W/mm)',
  'Breakdown<br>Voltage<br>(V)',
  'Thermal<br>Conductivity<br>(W/m·K)',
  'Efficiency<br>(%)',
  'Frequency<br>Range<br>(GHz)',
  'Relative<br>Cost<br>(lower=better)'
)

# Create radar/spider plot
fig <- plot_ly(
  type = 'scatterpolar',
  fill = 'toself'
)

# Add each technology as a separate trace
for (tech in tech_data) {
  # Close the polygon by repeating first value
  values_closed <- c(tech$normalized, tech$normalized[1])
  
  # Build detailed hover text with real values
  hover_text <- paste0(
    '<b>', tech$name, '</b><br>',
    '<br><b>Real Values:</b><br>',
    'Power Density: ', tech$real_values$power_density, '<br>',
    'Breakdown: ', tech$real_values$breakdown, '<br>',
    'Thermal: ', tech$real_values$thermal, '<br>',
    'Efficiency: ', tech$real_values$efficiency, '<br>',
    'Freq Range: Up to ', tech$real_values$freq_max, '<br>',
    'Cost: ', tech$real_values$relative_cost, '<br>',
    '<br>', tech$notes
  )
  
  fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
    r = values_closed,
    theta = c(categories, categories[1]),
    name = tech$name,
    line = list(color = tech$color, width = 2.5),
    fillcolor = paste0(tech$color, '30'),  # 30 = ~20% opacity in hex
    text = rep(hover_text, length(values_closed)),
    hoverinfo = 'text',
    hovertemplate = '%{text}<extra></extra>'
  )
}

# Layout configuration
fig <- fig %>% layout(
  polar = list(
    radialaxis = list(
      visible = TRUE,
      range = c(0, 10),
      tickmode = 'array',
      tickvals = c(0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10),
      ticktext = c('0', '2', '4', '6', '8', '10'),
      showline = TRUE,
      showgrid = TRUE,
      gridcolor = 'rgba(128,128,128,0.3)',
      tickfont = list(size = 10, color = '#666')
    ),
    angularaxis = list(
      tickfont = list(size = 10, color = '#333'),
      rotation = 75,  # Rotated to fit 6 dimensions
      direction = 'clockwise'
    ),
    bgcolor = 'rgba(245,245,245,0.5)'
  ),
  title = list(
    text = '<b>Technology Comparison: Power, Efficiency, and Cost</b><br><sub>Normalized scale (0-10 for visualization) • Hover for real values with units • Use toolbar to zoom/pan/reset</sub>',
    font = list(size = 14),
    y = 0.97
  ),
  showlegend = TRUE,
  legend = list(
    x = 1.08,
    y = 1,
    bgcolor = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.95)',
    bordercolor = 'gray',
    borderwidth = 1,
    font = list(size = 10.5)
  ),
  width = 1000,
  height = 850,
  margin = list(l = 100, r = 180, t = 120, b = 100),
  hovermode = 'closest'
)

# Enable modebar with all zoom/pan controls visible
fig <- fig %>% config(
  displayModeBar = TRUE,
  modeBarButtonsToRemove = list('select2d', 'lasso2d'),  # Keep zoom and pan buttons
  displaylogo = FALSE,
  toImageButtonOptions = list(
    format = 'png',
    filename = 'technology_comparison_spider',
    height = 850,
    width = 1000
  )
)

fig
```

<details class="technology-reference-table">
<summary style="cursor: pointer; font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.05em; padding: 10px; background-color: #E3F2FD; border-left: 4px solid #2196F3; margin: 15px 0;">📊 Click to expand: Real Values Reference Table & Metric Definitions</summary>

<div style="padding: 15px; background-color: #F5F5F5; border: 1px solid #ddd; margin-top: 5px;">

**Real Values Reference Table:**

| Technology | Power Density | Breakdown Voltage | Thermal Conductivity | Efficiency | Max Frequency | Relative Cost |
|:-----------|:-------------|:-----------------|:---------------------|:-----------|:--------------|:--------------|
| **GaN on Si** | 3–10 W/mm | 50–120 V | 130–150 W/m·K | 55–80% | ~40 GHz | Medium ($) |
| **GaN on SiC** | 8–20 W/mm | 100–200 V | 300–400 W/m·K | 60–85% | ~50 GHz | High ($$$) |
| **LDMOS** | 0.5–3 W/mm | 60–120 V | 130–150 W/m·K | 40–70% | ~3.5 GHz | Low ($$) |
| **GaAs** | 1–6 W/mm | 15–80 V | 40–50 W/m·K | 40–75% | ~100 GHz | Medium-High ($$) |
| **SiGe** | 0.5–2 W/mm | 5–20 V | 130–150 W/m·K | 30–60% | ~80 GHz | Low ($) |

---

**Metric Definitions:**

- **Power Density [W/mm]**: Output power per device gate periphery at optimum load impedance [@pengelly2012; @milligan2007]
- **Breakdown Voltage [V]**: Maximum drain-source voltage before catastrophic failure [@sze2006]
- **Thermal Conductivity [W/m·K]**: Substrate thermal conductivity; determines heat dissipation capability [@milligan2007]
- **Efficiency [%]**: Drain efficiency (DC to RF conversion); depends on PA class and bias [@raab2002]
- **Frequency Range [GHz]**: Practical upper operating frequency where gain >10 dB available [@pengelly2012]
- **Relative Cost**: Wafer cost and process complexity; $ (low) to $$$ (high) [@wood2013; @zampardi2013]

</div>
</details>

**Key Design Guidance:**

1. **Scale by area, not just device rating**: Deliverable output power is generally increased by paralleling device area; thermal path and matching networks become limiting factors before intrinsic device limits.

2. **Voltage vs. frequency trade-off**: Higher breakdown devices (GaN on SiC, LDMOS) enable larger $V_{DD}$ and therefore higher output power at lower frequencies; at mmWave, device parasitics and transit time dominate, favouring technologies with faster carriers even if voltage is lower.

3. **Thermal management is critical**: Substrate thermal conductivity (SiC >> Si >> GaAs) and package thermal resistance often determine continuous power capability and reliability. GaN on SiC excels due to superior heat dissipation.

4. **Efficiency depends on PA class and linearization**: Comparing raw device efficiency is misleading unless the intended PA class (A/AB/B/C/D/E/F) and linearization (DPD, envelope tracking) strategy are considered.

5. **Practical recommendation**: 
   - Use **LDMOS** for high-power, lower-frequency base stations (<3 GHz) where cost and ruggedness matter
   - Use **GaN on SiC** for highest power density and thermal headroom (defense, base stations)
   - Use **GaN on Si** for cost-sensitive but high-frequency designs (commercial 5G)
   - Use **GaAs/SiGe** for specialized microwave/linear or integrated front-ends (receivers, small-signal)

**Detailed Technology Characteristics:**

| Technology | Power Density | Breakdown Voltage | Thermal Conductivity | Typical Efficiency | Practical Bands |
|:-----------|:--------------|:------------------|:--------------------|:-------------------|:----------------|
| **GaN on Si** | 3–10 W/mm | 50–120 V | Si: ~130–150 W/m·K | 55–80% | VHF → mmWave |
| **GaN on SiC** | 8–20 W/mm | 100–200 V | SiC: ~300–400 W/m·K | 60–85% (best) | VHF → mmWave |
| **LDMOS** | 0.5–3 W/mm | 60–120 V | Si: ~130–150 W/m·K | 40–70% | HF → UHF |
| **GaAs** | 1–6 W/mm | 15–80 V | GaAs: ~40–50 W/m·K | 40–75% | µWave → mmWave |
| **SiGe** | 0.5–2 W/mm | 5–20 V | Si: ~130–150 W/m·K | 30–60% | Up to mmWave |

---

## DC Characteristics

### I-V Curves: Output Characteristics

```{r iv-curves-gan-hemt, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Figure 1.3: Interactive GaN HEMT Output Characteristics with PA Class Load Lines [@cripps2006; @colantonio2009]", fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
# Generate realistic GaN HEMT I-V curves with interactive Plotly
library(plotly)

# VDS sweep - extended range for log scale visibility
VDS <- seq(0.1, 120, 0.5)  # Start at 0.1 to avoid log(0)

# Gate voltages (depletion mode, negative Vth)
VGS_values <- seq(-5.5, 0, 0.5)

# Device parameters for 12mm GaN device (similar to DOE1)
Vth <- -5.5  # Threshold voltage
Vknee <- 4   # Knee voltage
Idss <- 800  # mA at VGS=0
lambda <- 0.01  # Channel length modulation
BVdss <- 100   # Breakdown voltage

# Generate ID curves for each VGS
iv_data <- data.frame()

for(VGS in VGS_values) {
  # Current calculation
  if(VGS < Vth) {
    ID <- rep(0, length(VDS))  # Below threshold
  } else {
    # Normalized transconductance factor
    gm_factor <- (VGS - Vth) / (0 - Vth)  # 0 to 1 as VGS goes from Vth to 0
    
    # Linear region (VDS < Vknee)
    ID_linear <- Idss * gm_factor * (VDS / Vknee) * (1 - 0.5 * VDS / Vknee)
    
    # Saturation region (VDS >= Vknee)
    ID_sat <- Idss * gm_factor^2 * (1 + lambda * (VDS - Vknee))
    
    # Combine regions
    ID <- ifelse(VDS < Vknee, pmax(ID_linear, 0), ID_sat)
    
    # Breakdown at high VDS (soft breakdown)
    ID <- ifelse(VDS > BVdss, ID * (1 + 0.05*(VDS - BVdss)), ID)
  }
  
  temp_df <- data.frame(
    VDS = VDS,
    ID = ID,
    VGS = VGS
  )
  
  iv_data <- rbind(iv_data, temp_df)
}

# Create base Plotly figure
fig <- plot_ly()

# Add I-V curves for each VGS
for(VGS_val in VGS_values) {
  subset_data <- iv_data[iv_data$VGS == VGS_val, ]
  fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
    x = subset_data$VDS,
    y = subset_data$ID,
    type = 'scatter',
    mode = 'lines',
    name = paste0('VGS = ', VGS_val, ' V'),
    line = list(width = 2),
    hovertemplate = paste('<b>VGS =', VGS_val, 'V</b><br>',
                          'VDS: %{x:.1f} V<br>',
                          'ID: %{y:.0f} mA<br>',
                          '<extra></extra>')
  )
}

# ========== PA CLASS LOAD LINES ==========

# Supply voltage
VDD <- 48  # Typical base station PA supply

# Class A Load Line: Maximum linear swing
# Q-point at (VDD/2, Idss/2), swings to (Vknee, Idss) and (VDD, 0)
class_A_VDS <- c(Vknee, VDD/2, VDD)
class_A_ID <- c(Idss * 0.9, Idss * 0.45, 0)
fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
  x = class_A_VDS,
  y = class_A_ID,
  type = 'scatter',
  mode = 'lines+markers',
  name = 'Class A Load Line',
  line = list(color = '#FF6F00', width = 3, dash = 'dash'),
  marker = list(size = 10, symbol = 'circle'),
  visible = 'legendonly',  # Hidden by default
  hovertemplate = '<b>Class A</b><br>VDS: %{x:.1f} V<br>ID: %{y:.0f} mA<br><extra></extra>'
)

# Class AB Load Line: Similar to Class A but lower quiescent current
class_AB_VDS <- c(Vknee, VDD/2, VDD)
class_AB_ID <- c(Idss * 0.85, Idss * 0.25, 0)
fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
  x = class_AB_VDS,
  y = class_AB_ID,
  type = 'scatter',
  mode = 'lines+markers',
  name = 'Class AB Load Line',
  line = list(color = '#4CAF50', width = 3, dash = 'dashdot'),
  marker = list(size = 10, symbol = 'square'),
  visible = 'legendonly',
  hovertemplate = '<b>Class AB</b><br>VDS: %{x:.1f} V<br>ID: %{y:.0f} mA<br>Quiescent: 25% Idss<extra></extra>'
)

# Class B Load Line: Biased at threshold, full swing
class_B_VDS <- c(Vknee, VDD)
class_B_ID <- c(Idss * 0.7, 0)
fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
  x = class_B_VDS,
  y = class_B_ID,
  type = 'scatter',
  mode = 'lines+markers',
  name = 'Class B Load Line',
  line = list(color = '#2196F3', width = 3, dash = 'dot'),
  marker = list(size = 10, symbol = 'diamond'),
  visible = 'legendonly',
  hovertemplate = '<b>Class B</b><br>VDS: %{x:.1f} V<br>ID: %{y:.0f} mA<br>Quiescent: 0 mA<extra></extra>'
)

# Class C Load Line: Below threshold, overdriven, higher VDD swing
class_C_VDS <- c(Vknee, VDD * 1.1)
class_C_ID <- c(Idss * 0.5, 0)
fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
  x = class_C_VDS,
  y = class_C_ID,
  type = 'scatter',
  mode = 'lines+markers',
  name = 'Class C Load Line',
  line = list(color = '#9C27B0', width = 3, dash = 'longdash'),
  marker = list(size = 10, symbol = 'triangle-up'),
  visible = 'legendonly',
  hovertemplate = '<b>Class C</b><br>VDS: %{x:.1f} V<br>ID: %{y:.0f} mA<br>Conduction angle: <180°<extra></extra>'
)

# Class D/E/F: Switching mode - vertical transitions (not traditional load line)
# Show switch trajectory: ON (Vknee, Idss) to OFF (VDD, 0)
class_D_VDS <- c(Vknee, Vknee, VDD, VDD)
class_D_ID <- c(0, Idss * 0.8, Idss * 0.8, 0)
fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
  x = class_D_VDS,
  y = class_D_ID,
  type = 'scatter',
  mode = 'lines+markers',
  name = 'Class D (Switching)',
  line = list(color = '#FF5722', width = 3, dash = 'solid'),
  marker = list(size = 10, symbol = 'x'),
  visible = 'legendonly',
  hovertemplate = '<b>Class D Switching</b><br>VDS: %{x:.1f} V<br>ID: %{y:.0f} mA<br>Mode: ON/OFF<extra></extra>'
)

# Class E: Optimized switching with ZVS, shows zero voltage switching trajectory
class_E_VDS <- c(0.5, Vknee, VDD * 1.2, VDD * 1.8, 0.5)
class_E_ID <- c(Idss * 0.6, Idss * 0.7, Idss * 0.2, 0, Idss * 0.6)
fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
  x = class_E_VDS,
  y = class_E_ID,
  type = 'scatter',
  mode = 'lines+markers',
  name = 'Class E (ZVS)',
  line = list(color = '#E91E63', width = 3, dash = 'dashdot'),
  marker = list(size = 10, symbol = 'star'),
  visible = 'legendonly',
  hovertemplate = '<b>Class E ZVS</b><br>VDS: %{x:.1f} V<br>ID: %{y:.0f} mA<br>Zero Voltage Switching<extra></extra>'
)

# Class F: Harmonic tuning, shows fundamental + 3rd harmonic trajectory
class_F_VDS <- seq(Vknee, VDD * 1.5, length.out = 30)
class_F_ID <- Idss * 0.4 * (1 + 0.6*sin(2*pi*seq(0,1,length.out=30))) * exp(-0.5*seq(0,1,length.out=30))
fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
  x = class_F_VDS,
  y = pmax(class_F_ID, 0),
  type = 'scatter',
  mode = 'lines+markers',
  name = 'Class F (Harmonic)',
  line = list(color = '#00BCD4', width = 3, dash = 'dot'),
  marker = list(size = 8, symbol = 'hexagon'),
  visible = 'legendonly',
  hovertemplate = '<b>Class F Harmonic</b><br>VDS: %{x:.1f} V<br>ID: %{y:.0f} mA<br>Harmonic tuning<extra></extra>'
)

# Add reference lines
fig <- fig %>%
  # Vknee line
  add_segments(x = Vknee, xend = Vknee, y = 0, yend = 900,
               line = list(color = 'gray', width = 1, dash = 'dash'),
               name = 'Vknee', showlegend = FALSE,
               hovertemplate = paste('Vknee =', Vknee, 'V<extra></extra>')) %>%
  # BVdss line
  add_segments(x = BVdss, xend = BVdss, y = 0, yend = 900,
               line = list(color = 'red', width = 2, dash = 'dash'),
               name = 'BVdss', showlegend = FALSE,
               hovertemplate = paste('BVdss =', BVdss, 'V (AVOID!)<extra></extra>'))

# Layout with log X-axis, linear Y-axis, and fixed dimensions
fig <- fig %>% layout(
  title = list(
    text = '<b>GaN HEMT Output Characteristics: ID vs VDS (Interactive)</b><br><sub>12mm device with PA Class Load Lines - Toggle legend to show/hide</sub>',
    font = list(size = 16)
  ),
  xaxis = list(
    title = 'Drain-Source Voltage VDS (V)',
    type = 'log',  # LOG SCALE X-axis for knee voltage visibility
    range = c(log10(0.1), log10(120)),
    gridcolor = 'rgba(128,128,128,0.2)'
  ),
  yaxis = list(
    title = 'Drain Current ID (mA)',
    range = c(0, 900),  # LINEAR SCALE Y-axis
    gridcolor = 'rgba(128,128,128,0.2)'
  ),
  autosize = FALSE,
  width = 1000,
  height = 600,
  hovermode = 'closest',
  legend = list(
    x = 1.02,
    y = 1,
    bgcolor = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.8)',
    bordercolor = 'gray',
    borderwidth = 1
  ),
  plot_bgcolor = 'white',
  margin = list(l = 80, r = 150, t = 100, b = 80),
  annotations = list(
    # Linear region annotation
    list(x = log10(1), y = 850, text = '<b>Linear Region</b>',
         showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 12, color = '#1976D2')),
    # Saturation region annotation
    list(x = log10(25), y = 850, text = '<b>Saturation (PA Operation)</b>',
         showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 12, color = '#388E3C')),
    # Breakdown region annotation
    list(x = log10(108), y = 850, text = '<b>Breakdown (AVOID!)</b>',
         showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 12, color = '#D32F2F'))
  )
)

fig
```

**Key Features to Extract:**

| Parameter | Definition | Typical Value | Significance |
|-----------|------------|---------------|--------------|
| $I_{DSS}$ | Drain current at $V_{GS}=0$ | 50-100 mA/mm | Maximum current capability |
| $V_P$ (Pinch-off) | $V_{GS}$ where $I_D \approx 0$ | -3 to -6 V | Device normally-on/off |
| $BV_{DSS}$ | Drain-source breakdown | 80-200 V | Operating voltage limit |
| $R_{DSon}$ | On-resistance | 2-10 Ω·mm | Conduction loss |
| $V_{knee}$ | Saturation onset | 3-7 V | Efficiency limit |

---

### **Understanding the Load Line: Why VDS Decreases When Input Increases**

The load line represents the **constraint imposed by the external circuit** on the transistor's operation. Many students find it counterintuitive that VDS decreases when the input signal increases. Here's the physical explanation:

### The Load Line Equation

For a transistor with a resistive load ($R_L$) connected between the drain and supply voltage ($V_{DD}$):

$$V_{DS} = V_{DD} - I_D \times R_L$$

**Key insight**: This is Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL) around the drain circuit.

### Physical Operation During RF Cycle

1. **Input Signal Swings HIGH** → $V_{GS}$ increases:
   - Transistor channel conducts **more** → $I_D$ **increases**
   - More current through load resistor → Voltage drop across $R_L$ **increases**
   - By KVL: $V_{DS} = V_{DD} - I_D R_L$ → $V_{DS}$ **decreases**
   - Operating point moves **down and left** along load line

2. **Input Signal Swings LOW** → $V_{GS}$ decreases:
   - Transistor channel conducts **less** → $I_D$ **decreases**  
   - Less current through load resistor → Voltage drop across $R_L$ **decreases**
   - By KVL: $V_{DS} = V_{DD} - I_D R_L$ → $V_{DS}$ **increases**
   - Operating point moves **up and right** along load line

### Load Line Characteristics

- **Slope** = $-1/R_L$ (always negative)
- **Y-intercept** (VDS = 0): $I_D = V_{DD}/R_L$ (short-circuit current)
- **X-intercept** (ID = 0): $V_{DS} = V_{DD}$ (transistor OFF)

**Optimal Load Resistance Selection:**
- Too small $R_L$ → Steep slope → Large current swing, small voltage swing → Current-limited
- Too large $R_L$ → Shallow slope → Large voltage swing, small current swing → Voltage-limited
- **Optimal** $R_L$ → Maximizes output power swing while staying within safe operating area

### The Voltage Inversion Concept

This is the **fundamental mechanism of voltage amplification**:
- Input signal modulates gate voltage: $v_{in}(t) = V_{GS,Q} + v_{gs}(t)$
- Transistor converts this to drain current: $i_D(t) = I_{D,Q} + i_d(t)$
- Load resistor converts current to voltage: $v_{DS}(t) = V_{DD} - i_D(t) \times R_L$
- **Result**: Output voltage is **180° out of phase** with drain current
- This is why common-source amplifiers are **inverting** amplifiers

### Load Line vs. Dynamic Load Line

**DC Load Line** (what we typically plot):
- Assumes purely resistive load
- Straight line from $(V_{DD}, 0)$ to $(V_{knee}, I_{max})$

**Dynamic Load Line** (actual RF operation):
- Includes reactive components from matching networks
- Can be elliptical or distorted due to $C$ and $L$ elements
- Changes with frequency and harmonic content
- **Load-pull measurements** map the actual dynamic impedance

```{r load-line-animation, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Figure 1.3b: Load Line Operation - How Operating Point Moves During RF Cycle", fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)

# Define operating parameters
VDD <- 48      # Supply voltage (V)
Vknee <- 4     # Knee voltage (V)
Idss <- 800    # Maximum current (mA)
Rload <- 55    # Load resistance (Ohms)

# Calculate load line endpoints
VDS_max <- VDD
ID_max <- (VDD - Vknee) / Rload  # Max current at knee voltage
VDS_loadline <- seq(Vknee, VDD, length.out = 50)
ID_loadline <- (VDD - VDS_loadline) / Rload

# Quiescent point (Class AB example)
VDS_Q <- VDD / 1.8  # ~27V
ID_Q <- (VDD - VDS_Q) / Rload  # Calculate using load line

# Generate I-V curves for multiple VGS values
VDS_curve <- seq(0.1, 100, length.out = 200)
VGS_values <- seq(-6, 0, 0.5)
Vth <- -5.5

# Calculate ID for each VGS
iv_curves <- data.frame()
for (VGS_val in VGS_values) {
  if (VGS_val <= Vth) {
    ID_curve <- rep(0, length(VDS_curve))
  } else {
    # Simplified GaN HEMT model
    gm_factor <- ((VGS_val - Vth) / (0 - Vth))^1.8
    ID_sat <- Idss * gm_factor
    
    # Triode and saturation regions
    ID_curve <- ifelse(VDS_curve < Vknee,
                       ID_sat * (VDS_curve / Vknee),  # Linear/triode
                       ID_sat * (1 + 0.015 * (VDS_curve - Vknee)))  # Saturation with slight slope
    
    # Current limiting near breakdown
    ID_curve <- ifelse(VDS_curve > 90, ID_sat * 0.95, ID_curve)
  }
  
  iv_curves <- rbind(iv_curves, data.frame(
    VDS = VDS_curve,
    ID = ID_curve,
    VGS = VGS_val
  ))
}

# Find which VGS curve passes through Q-point
VGS_Q <- -2.8  # Approximate for Class AB

# Generate RF signal trajectory (sinusoidal modulation)
n_points <- 50
theta <- seq(0, 2*pi, length.out = n_points)

# Input signal swing (±0.8V around VGS_Q)
VGS_swing <- 0.8
VGS_rf <- VGS_Q + VGS_swing * sin(theta)

# Calculate corresponding ID and VDS using load line
ID_rf <- numeric(n_points)
VDS_rf <- numeric(n_points)

for (i in 1:n_points) {
  # Calculate ID from transfer characteristic
  if (VGS_rf[i] <= Vth) {
    ID_rf[i] <- 0
  } else {
    gm_factor <- ((VGS_rf[i] - Vth) / (0 - Vth))^1.8
    ID_rf[i] <- Idss * gm_factor
  }
  
  # Calculate VDS from load line equation
  VDS_rf[i] <- VDD - ID_rf[i] * Rload
  
  # Ensure we stay in saturation region
  if (VDS_rf[i] < Vknee) VDS_rf[i] <- Vknee
  if (VDS_rf[i] > VDD) VDS_rf[i] <- VDD
}

# Create plot
fig <- plot_ly()

# Add I-V curves (lighter, in background)
for (VGS_val in VGS_values) {
  curve_data <- iv_curves %>% filter(VGS == VGS_val)
  fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
    x = curve_data$VDS,
    y = curve_data$ID,
    type = 'scatter',
    mode = 'lines',
    line = list(color = 'rgba(150, 150, 150, 0.3)', width = 1),
    name = paste0('VGS = ', VGS_val, ' V'),
    legendgroup = 'iv_curves',
    showlegend = FALSE,
    hoverinfo = 'skip'
  )
}

# Add DC load line (thick red dashed)
fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
  x = VDS_loadline,
  y = ID_loadline,
  type = 'scatter',
  mode = 'lines',
  name = paste0('DC Load Line (RL = ', Rload, ' Ω)'),
  line = list(color = '#D32F2F', width = 4, dash = 'dash'),
  hovertemplate = paste0('<b>DC Load Line</b><br>',
                        'Slope = -1/RL = -', round(1/Rload, 4), ' A/V<br>',
                        'VDS: %{x:.1f} V<br>',
                        'ID: %{y:.0f} mA<br>',
                        '<extra></extra>')
)

# Add Q-point (large marker)
fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
  x = VDS_Q,
  y = ID_Q,
  type = 'scatter',
  mode = 'markers+text',
  name = 'Quiescent Point (Q)',
  marker = list(color = '#FF6F00', size = 20, symbol = 'star',
                line = list(color = 'white', width = 2)),
  text = 'Q-point',
  textposition = 'top center',
  textfont = list(size = 14, color = '#FF6F00', family = 'Arial Black'),
  hovertemplate = paste0('<b>Quiescent Point (Q)</b><br>',
                        'VDS,Q = ', round(VDS_Q, 1), ' V<br>',
                        'ID,Q = ', round(ID_Q, 0), ' mA<br>',
                        'VGS,Q = ', VGS_Q, ' V<br>',
                        '<i>Class AB bias</i><br>',
                        '<extra></extra>')
)

# Add RF trajectory (thick blue line with arrows)
fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
  x = VDS_rf,
  y = ID_rf,
  type = 'scatter',
  mode = 'lines+markers',
  name = 'RF Signal Trajectory',
  line = list(color = '#1976D2', width = 3),
  marker = list(size = 6, color = '#1976D2'),
  hovertemplate = paste0('<b>RF Operating Point</b><br>',
                        'VDS: %{x:.1f} V<br>',
                        'ID: %{y:.0f} mA<br>',
                        '<extra></extra>')
)

# Add directional arrows to show movement
# Peak current point (input HIGH)
peak_current_idx <- which.max(ID_rf)
fig <- fig %>% add_annotations(
  x = VDS_rf[peak_current_idx],
  y = ID_rf[peak_current_idx],
  text = '<b>Input HIGH</b><br>ID max<br>VDS min',
  showarrow = TRUE,
  arrowhead = 2,
  arrowsize = 1.5,
  arrowwidth = 3,
  arrowcolor = '#E91E63',
  ax = -80,
  ay = -60,
  font = list(size = 12, color = '#E91E63', family = 'Arial'),
  bgcolor = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.9)',
  bordercolor = '#E91E63',
  borderwidth = 2
)

# Peak voltage point (input LOW)
peak_voltage_idx <- which.max(VDS_rf)
fig <- fig %>% add_annotations(
  x = VDS_rf[peak_voltage_idx],
  y = ID_rf[peak_voltage_idx],
  text = '<b>Input LOW</b><br>ID min<br>VDS max',
  showarrow = TRUE,
  arrowhead = 2,
  arrowsize = 1.5,
  arrowwidth = 3,
  arrowcolor = '#4CAF50',
  ax = 60,
  ay = 60,
  font = list(size = 12, color = '#4CAF50', family = 'Arial'),
  bgcolor = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.9)',
  bordercolor = '#4CAF50',
  borderwidth = 2
)

# Add voltage swing indicator
fig <- fig %>% add_annotations(
  x = mean(range(VDS_rf)),
  y = max(ID_rf) * 1.15,
  text = paste0('<b>Voltage Swing</b><br>ΔV<sub>DS</sub> = ', 
                round(max(VDS_rf) - min(VDS_rf), 1), ' V<sub>pp</sub>'),
  showarrow = FALSE,
  font = list(size = 14, color = '#1976D2'),
  bgcolor = 'rgba(25, 118, 210, 0.1)',
  bordercolor = '#1976D2',
  borderwidth = 2
)

# Add current swing indicator  
fig <- fig %>% add_annotations(
  x = VDD * 1.05,
  y = mean(range(ID_rf)),
  text = paste0('<b>Current Swing</b><br>ΔI<sub>D</sub> = ', 
                round(max(ID_rf) - min(ID_rf), 0), ' mA<sub>pp</sub>'),
  showarrow = FALSE,
  font = list(size = 14, color = '#D32F2F'),
  bgcolor = 'rgba(211, 47, 47, 0.1)',
  bordercolor = '#D32F2F',
  borderwidth = 2
)

# Layout
fig <- fig %>% layout(
  title = list(
    text = '<b>Load Line Operation: RF Signal Trajectory</b><br><sub>Shows how operating point moves along load line during RF cycle</sub>',
    font = list(size = 16)
  ),
  xaxis = list(
    title = '<b>V<sub>DS</sub> (Drain-Source Voltage) [V]</b>',
    range = c(0, VDD * 1.15),
    gridcolor = 'rgba(128,128,128,0.2)'
  ),
  yaxis = list(
    title = '<b>I<sub>D</sub> (Drain Current) [mA]</b>',
    range = c(0, max(ID_rf) * 1.3),
    gridcolor = 'rgba(128,128,128,0.2)'
  ),
  width = 1000,
  height = 700,
  hovermode = 'closest',
  legend = list(
    x = 0.02,
    y = 0.98,
    bgcolor = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.9)',
    bordercolor = 'gray',
    borderwidth = 1
  ),
  plot_bgcolor = 'white',
  margin = list(l = 80, r = 120, t = 100, b = 80)
)

fig
```

**Key Observations from Load Line Animation:**

1. **Operating Point Movement:**
   - Moves along the **DC load line** during RF operation
   - **Anti-phase relationship**: When $I_D$ ↑, then $V_{DS}$ ↓ (and vice versa)
   - Forms a trajectory that follows: $V_{DS}(t) = V_{DD} - I_D(t) \times R_L$

2. **Voltage Amplification:**
   - Input swing: ~1.6 V peak-to-peak at gate
   - Output swing: ~30-40 V peak-to-peak at drain
   - **Voltage gain** = (Output swing) / (Input swing) ≈ 20-25×

3. **Power Output:**
   - RF output power ∝ $\frac{1}{2} \Delta V_{DS} \times \Delta I_D$
   - Maximized when operating point swings symmetrically
   - Limited by $V_{knee}$ (lower bound) and $V_{DD}$ (upper bound)

4. **Class-Dependent Behavior:**
   - **Class A**: Q-point at center, symmetric swing, most linear
   - **Class AB**: Q-point below center (shown), good linearity + efficiency
   - **Class B**: Q-point at cutoff, half-waveform conduction
   - Each class has different Q-point placement on the load line

---

### Integrated PA Class Visualization: Load Lines and Waveforms

This comprehensive visualization shows the relationship between I-V load lines and corresponding input/output waveforms for different PA classes, providing a unified view of transistor operation.

```{r pa-class-integrated-visualization, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Figure 1.3c: Integrated PA Class Visualization - Load Lines and Input/Output Waveforms",fig.width=11, fig.height=8.5}
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)

# ========== DEVICE PARAMETERS ==========
VDD <- 48       # Supply voltage (V)
Vknee <- 4      # Knee voltage (V)
Idss <- 800     # Maximum current (mA)
Vth <- -5.5     # Threshold voltage (V)
Rload <- 55     # Load resistance (Ohms)
BVdss <- 100    # Breakdown voltage

# ========== GENERATE I-V CURVES ==========
VDS_curve <- seq(0.1, 100, 0.5)
VGS_values <- seq(-5.5, 0, 0.5)

iv_data <- data.frame()
for(VGS in VGS_values) {
  if(VGS < Vth) {
    ID <- rep(0, length(VDS_curve))
  } else {
    gm_factor <- (VGS - Vth) / (0 - Vth)
    ID_linear <- Idss * gm_factor * (VDS_curve / Vknee) * (1 - 0.5 * VDS_curve / Vknee)
    ID_sat <- Idss * gm_factor^2 * (1 + 0.01 * (VDS_curve - Vknee))
    ID <- ifelse(VDS_curve < Vknee, pmax(ID_linear, 0), ID_sat)
    ID <- ifelse(VDS_curve > BVdss, ID * (1 + 0.05*(VDS_curve - BVdss)), ID)
  }
  iv_data <- rbind(iv_data, data.frame(VDS = VDS_curve, ID = ID, VGS = VGS))
}

# ========== TIME DOMAIN WAVEFORMS ==========
t <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 200)
omega <- 2 * pi

# CLASS A: Full 360° conduction
VGS_Q_A <- -2.8
VGS_swing_A <- 0.8
VGS_A <- VGS_Q_A + VGS_swing_A * sin(omega * t)
gm_factor_A <- ((VGS_A - Vth) / (0 - Vth))^1.8
ID_A <- pmax(Idss * gm_factor_A, 0)
VDS_A <- pmax(pmin(VDD - ID_A * Rload, VDD), Vknee)

# CLASS AB: ~180-270° conduction
VGS_Q_AB <- -4.2
VGS_swing_AB <- 1.2
VGS_AB <- VGS_Q_AB + VGS_swing_AB * sin(omega * t)
gm_factor_AB <- ((VGS_AB - Vth) / (0 - Vth))^1.8
ID_AB <- pmax(Idss * gm_factor_AB, 0)
VDS_AB <- pmax(pmin(VDD - ID_AB * Rload, VDD), Vknee)

# CLASS B: 180° conduction
VGS_Q_B <- Vth
VGS_swing_B <- 1.5
VGS_B <- VGS_Q_B + VGS_swing_B * sin(omega * t)
gm_factor_B <- ((VGS_B - Vth) / (0 - Vth))^1.8
ID_B <- pmax(Idss * gm_factor_B, 0)
VDS_B <- pmax(pmin(VDD - ID_B * Rload, VDD), Vknee)

# CLASS C: <180° conduction
VGS_Q_C <- Vth - 1.0
VGS_swing_C <- 2.0
VGS_C <- VGS_Q_C + VGS_swing_C * sin(omega * t)
gm_factor_C <- ((VGS_C - Vth) / (0 - Vth))^1.8
ID_C <- pmax(Idss * gm_factor_C, 0)
VDS_C <- pmax(pmin(VDD - ID_C * Rload, VDD * 1.3), Vknee)

# CLASS D: Switching mode
VGS_Q_D <- Vth - 2.0
VGS_swing_D <- 3.0
VGS_D <- VGS_Q_D + VGS_swing_D * sin(omega * t)
ID_D <- ifelse(VGS_D > Vth, Idss * 0.7, 0)
VDS_D <- ifelse(ID_D > 0, Vknee, VDD)

# CLASS E: ZVS
VGS_Q_E <- Vth - 2.0
VGS_swing_E <- 3.5
VGS_E <- VGS_Q_E + VGS_swing_E * sin(omega * t)
ID_E <- ifelse(VGS_E > Vth, Idss * 0.6, 0)
phase_shift <- pi/6
VDS_E <- pmax(pmin(VDD * (1 + 0.8 * sin(omega * t + phase_shift)), VDD * 1.8), 0)

# CLASS F: Harmonic tuning
VGS_Q_F <- Vth - 1.5
VGS_swing_F <- 2.5
VGS_F <- VGS_Q_F + VGS_swing_F * sin(omega * t)
gm_factor_F <- ((VGS_F - Vth) / (0 - Vth))^1.8
ID_F <- pmax(Idss * gm_factor_F * 0.7, 0)
VDS_F <- pmax(pmin(VDD * (1 + 0.6 * sin(omega * t) + 0.2 * sin(3 * omega * t)), VDD * 1.5), Vknee)

# ========== LOAD LINE TRAJECTORIES ==========
class_A_VDS <- c(Vknee, VDD/2, VDD)
class_A_ID <- c(Idss * 0.9, Idss * 0.45, 0)

class_AB_VDS <- c(Vknee, VDD/2, VDD)
class_AB_ID <- c(Idss * 0.85, Idss * 0.25, 0)

class_B_VDS <- c(Vknee, VDD)
class_B_ID <- c(Idss * 0.7, 0)

class_C_VDS <- c(Vknee, VDD * 1.1)
class_C_ID <- c(Idss * 0.5, 0)

class_D_VDS <- c(Vknee, Vknee, VDD, VDD)
class_D_ID <- c(0, Idss * 0.8, Idss * 0.8, 0)

class_E_VDS <- c(0.5, Vknee, VDD * 1.2, VDD * 1.8, 0.5)
class_E_ID <- c(Idss * 0.6, Idss * 0.7, Idss * 0.2, 0, Idss * 0.6)

class_F_VDS <- seq(Vknee, VDD * 1.5, length.out = 30)
class_F_ID <- pmax(Idss * 0.4 * (1 + 0.6*sin(2*pi*seq(0,1,length.out=30))) * exp(-0.5*seq(0,1,length.out=30)), 0)

# ========== CREATE SUBPLOT 1: I-V CURVES WITH LOAD LINES ==========
p1 <- plot_ly()

# Add I-V curves (background, lighter)
for(VGS_val in VGS_values) {
  curve_data <- iv_data %>% filter(VGS == VGS_val)
  p1 <- p1 %>% add_trace(
    x = curve_data$VDS, y = curve_data$ID,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
    line = list(width = 1, color = 'rgba(100,100,100,0.3)'),
    name = paste0('VGS=', VGS_val, 'V'),
    showlegend = FALSE,
    hovertemplate = paste0('VGS=', VGS_val, 'V<br>VDS: %{x:.1f}V<br>ID: %{y:.0f}mA<extra></extra>')
  )
}

# Add load lines for each class
p1 <- p1 %>%
  add_trace(x = class_A_VDS, y = class_A_ID, type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines+markers',
            name = 'Class A', legendgroup = 'classA',
            line = list(color = '#FF6F00', width = 3, dash = 'dash'),
            marker = list(size = 8, symbol = 'circle'),
            hovertemplate = '<b>Class A</b><br>VDS: %{x:.1f}V<br>ID: %{y:.0f}mA<extra></extra>') %>%
  add_trace(x = class_AB_VDS, y = class_AB_ID, type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines+markers',
            name = 'Class AB', legendgroup = 'classAB', visible = 'legendonly',
            line = list(color = '#4CAF50', width = 3, dash = 'dashdot'),
